questions.text: When taking the earpiece off the hook, and there is no dial tone coming from the earpiece this indicates that the line is not busy, and you would be able to make a call

answers.1: It is important to show empathy towards customers so that they can feel understood and that is also building trust and rapport.
answers.2: The first thing is to ask the customer if their CPE is powered on, if no, ask them to do a power cycle, then you can also check if the customer's account is active and not suspended on CRM, after you can check if they are not experiencing any network outages on Chip.
answers.3: The next step is to confirm the installation in question and check if it is also affected on Preseem, if only one installation is affected then we can escalate to fields operations but if multiple installations are affected then I will need to escalate to config & analyze.
answers.4: To confirm if the cables are plugged in from the correct ports, and if the power cable is plugged in to the correct slot.
answers.5: The next is to do the power cycle, if after the power cycle the red flashing light is still on, I will go check on Preseem how many customers have lost signal, and if I see it is only my customer then I will escalate to fields operations but if multiple then I will escalate to config & analayze
answers.7: The STP cable is plugged incorrectly, it must be plugged in by the PoE port on the PoE and the ethernet cable leading to the gateway should be plugged in by the WAN port.
answers.8: To check if there is a communication between the ODU and the AP by logging in to the customers AP if the AP does not respond then log into the NAS if that too does not respond then I will escalate to config & analyze.
answers.11: The ethernet cable from the ONT should be connected to the blue port on the TP link Archer C5 not the yellow port.
answers.14: The equipment shown is a ONT/ONU
answers.16: These indicates that the router has reset to default settings that is why it is showing provisioning on ACS
answers.17: The customers Wi-Fi on their device is offline, they need to switch it on then search for the Wi-Fi name and try to connect using their Wi-Fi password.

questions.text: Scenario : You are in the process of assisting a customer via whatsapp that is stating that when the browse on there laptop it states this site cannot be reached. During troubleshooting the customer sends you the attached screen shot. What is the cause of the problem ?
questions.adminNotes: • The customers Wi-Fi is disabled.

answers.0: The possible cause here could be that those trees are blocking the signal that is suppose to connect the customer from the ODU to the CPE. This means when there is an obstruction in front of the antenna, customer might struggle to receive the signal from their CPE.
answers.1: It indicates that the customer is reaching their full bandwidth package, but it might be that the reason why they are only downloading at 5 Mbps it is because the bandwidth is shared by other devices connected to that same network.
answers.2: They will have to buy an extender router that will connect from the original point to where the work shop is using the STP CAT 1-7 cable so that they can receive uninterrupted signal from the original point of installation.
answers.4: They should use the Shielded Twisted Pair Cable (STP) because it is suitable for outdoor installations.
answers.5: It could be that the cables are not plugged into the correct port from the ONT to the gateway that it hinders the internet connection between the customers device and the gateway, or it could be that there is a network outage affecting that fibre area of that customer that is why the customer is unable to have internet access.
answers.6: Because those customers are connected to that dome so once the dome has been removed, those customers connected to the same pole wont have internet access.
answers.7: There could be multiple interruptions that cause loss of signal and because they are using UTP it means that it can be easily affected by interferences. Also power cables can trigger electronic interference that can make the internet connection to be very slow.
answers.8: The possible cause of slow speed could be the distance first between from the access point and in-between the switches, other thing it could be because all those tenants are sharing the same bandwidth of the wifi package and that also can cause the speed to be very slow, or the ethernet cable that connects the tenants might be faulty too.
answers.9: This is a DHCP method and it might be that the packages are taking time to respond from the receiving end so the might be an issue with the IP lease time
answers.10: It should be configured in the gateway on ACS because that is where the port forwarding rule can be configured
answers.17: Using MAC address
answers.22:
Authentication Request to the Radius server was rejected due to one of multiple Reasons
Account and or pppoe username might be blocked from corteza
answers.24:
The username has a realm ("@bus.herotel.com") which is configured to not authenticate on the server but on a separate radius, in this case 
Hero Business. 
The authentication DOES NOT take place on the Radius but instead gets forwarded to the Hero Business Herotill instance. This is normal 
expected behaviour
answers.26: Account code
answers.29: The account is suspended
answers.31: The status is invalid and it needs to be escalated to config & analyze
answers.32: Check which C-panel the customer is under CP2 or CP1
answers.34: No they need to contact the customer at lease 4 times within during the shift
answers.36: Change the department assigned to and the problem statement
answers.37: Educate the customer about the usage send them to sales to increase package

questions.text:

questions.text: Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they have been expereincing slow speed for the past month and they wish to cancel. What is the correct procedure.

  • Escalate to a team leader if the problem is not resolved after assistance

answers.0: The possible cause of this problem is that the fibre light is above -27 which indicates that the fibre light of the customer showing -36 has bad fibre light and also the customers bandwidth might have been shared amongst other devices that are connected to the same network as the customer that is why they are not matching their package. So this will lead to the next step checking if this is only affecting the customer or there are multiple customers affected, if singular I will escalate to fields ops, if multiple then escalate to config & analyze.
answers.1: This metrics indicates that the customer is maxing out their bandwidth, so I would advise the customer to at least decrease the usage of his bandwidth by refraining from downloading and uploading huge files or streaming high frequency channels or encourage them to increase their package if needs be.
answers.2: This metrics shows that the is a level of bottle neck and the internet is dropping, the green line and yellow line are spiking too much and also the blue line is not stable meaning the latency is very bad so that might be a possible issue with the AP.
answers.3: This metrics indicates that the Health and RF score are bad meaning this indicates a problem between the AP and customers connected to the AP and needs to be escalated to config & analyze
answers.4: I would advise the customer to switch off all other devices that are connected to their network, and ask them to do the speed test standing 2 meters away from the router, and also advise them to do the speed test connecting to the 5Ghz frequency so that they can get the best accurate results.
answers.5: False, A fibre customer can perform the speed test and 2 minutes later the speed test results will populate, the 10 seconds speed test results would populate if this was a wireless customer.
answers.6: I would explain to the customer that when the speed test was showing less it was most probably because the was a device sharing his bandwidth or there is a hidden device that takes up the customers bandwidth hence it was not matching his full bandwidth.
answers.9: Wireless medium , and the blue line indicates an access point.
answers.10: If the ODU and the in-home is experiencing the high latency but the AP is stable it could indicate an out of spec ODU as well a damaged ODU but the sectors latency should be confirmed before coming to a conclusion.
answers.11: This throughput indicates that the customer is reaching his real time downlink and uplink package, but the uplink only went up to its full bandwidth at 13:32 not the same time as the downlink package.
answers.12: This shows a good health and RF score meaning this is indicating that there is most likely no issue between the sector and the customers ODU, so maybe the customer needs to check their devices.

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating they are expereincing slow speed. The following steps have been performed :

1. What is the possible cause of the ploblem ?
2. What is the next step ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The customers fibre light is more then -27
  • Confirm if all customer connected to the OLT & PON have bad fibre light or only the singular customer

questions.text:
Scenario : A fibre customer contacts in and states that they have been expereincing slow speed for the past week.  The following has been confirmed :
  • There were no reported degraded service outage in the past week
  • The customer confirms there children are on school vacation
  • The customer package is a 20 Mbos download & 10 Mbps upload wireelss package
  • The customer performed a speed test and the attached preseem metrics are the results.
1. What does the metrics indicate ?
2. How would you educate the customer on the problem and its cause ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The customer is utilizing there full bandwidth that the package offers
  • Agent educates the customer on how bandwidth works

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that there connection feels slow and at times very intermitted. You have performed the following troubleshooting steps :
  • The customer is not part of a outage affecting the AP they are connecting to
  • The customers RF score is 4.07 & ok
  • The customers power for the equipment is stable 
  • The preseem metrics are as attached
1. What do the metrics indicate ?
2. What is latency ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The customers ODU , in home & AP has high latency indicating a problm on the AP
  • Latency is how long packets take from point a to reach point b

questions.text:
During the troubleshooting process you open the AP metrics (find the metrics attached).
  • What do the metrics indicate ?
  • What is the next recommended step ?
questions.adminNotes:
• There is a problem on the Ap as both the health & RF score is bad.
  • Escalate to C & A

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in stating that when they perform a speed test via there computer connected with a ethernet cable then the speed is correct. But when they watch tv which is connected via wi-fi it buffers. Speed tests performed via the Wi-Fi is also significantly lower then the ethernet test. Keep in mind the customer has a 100 Mbps symetrical fibre connection.
What further steps can be performed with the customer to narrow down if the problem is via the Wi-Fi or if the network in general could be expereincing the problem ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Distance from device to router
  • Freqeuncy to which the device is connected

questions.text:
True / False : A fibre customer can perform a speed test and 10 seconds later the speed test results will populate in the metrics. 
  • Provide a reason for your answer.
questions.adminNotes:
• False
  • Fibre customers poll every 2 minutes as they have not gone inline

questions.text:
Scenario : You are in the process of guiding the customer to perform a speed test. After the speed test the preseem metrics shows that the customer was utilizing the full available bandwidth in that period how ever the speed test results shows less. 
  • How would you explain to the customer what is the cause of this discrepency between what you see and what the speed test result shows the customer ?
questions.adminNotes: • key points mentioned : Speed test only shows whats available not what is being utilized

questions.text: What Wi-Fi channel should a router be configured to for optimal performance ?
options: The channel does not influence the performance

questions.text: What is the acceptable fibre light level range
Which medium (service) provides a latency graph and what does the blue line on the graph indicate ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Wireless medium
  • The blue line represents the AP

questions.text:
Scenario : You are in the process of troubleshooting a customers slow speed problem. You have confirmed the following :The blue line on the latency graph on preseem is stableThe orange and green line on the latency graph on preseem is not stable and the latency readings show the ms is 200.
  • What does the graph indicate ?
questions.adminNotes: • The customers ODU could be damaged / out of alignment as only the customers in home and ODU'[s latency is hight but the AP is stable

questions.text: What does he following throughput graph from a customers antenna indicate. Note the customer has a 5 Mbps download and 2 mbps upload package.
questions.adminNotes: • The customer is either not using the full bandwith or the ODU is not receive the full bandwidth of the package

questions.text: Scenario : While performing AP troubleshooting steps you come accross attached metrcis. How ever the customer still states the speed is slow. The latency graph is normal and stable. Based on the attached metrics where is the problem possibly originating from ?
questions.adminNotes: • Either the sector is not getting bandwidth or the problem is in home

answers.0: The customers email domain is suspended the domains banner should be gray/white not red more information regarding the suspension can be viewed by pressing the plus sign and navigating to the reason for suspension
2: Select "Tools"
Select "Email Deliverability"
Wait for the deliverability status
Escalate to Config & Analyse if result is "Invalid"
5: Confirm if there are email address spaces available
Insert the customer’s desired email name (without domain)
answers.6: they should be escalated to a sales specialist for a package upgrade
9: Select “Generate” to create a new password
answers.10:
Check outlook for any bounce codes and other errors
checking outlooks bounce codes will provide you with possible causes that can help you troubleshoot the problem
answers.15: You can use a mail tester to check is the client can send mail
answers.16: the agent forgot to confirm if herotel hosts the clients domain using the herotel portal
answers.17:
domain          provider
Gmail.com       Google LLC
telkom.co.za    Telkom SA Soc Ltd
yahoo.co.za     Yahoo
mweb.co.za      Mweb (Pty) Ltd
answers.23: the email was using all of its quoted storage and this was causing it not to be able to send/receive mail because it did not have any storage to store the mail by increasing the emails quota we resolved the this problem
answers.24: wire guard

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that that they cannot send / receive. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customer does have internet access on the computer
  • The customers account is active
  • The screen shot below is the customers domain status
1. What is the cause of the problem the customer is expereincing & provide a reason for your answer ?
questions.adminNotes:
1.The domain is suspended 
1. The outer colour panel is red and there is a date noted by suspended

questions.text: Complete the sentance : If a domain has no storage space left, you must either educate the customer to delete emails or escalate to ___________ for a package upgrade.
questions.adminNotes: sales_specialist

questions.text: What is the correct order for performing a email deliverabiltiy check on C-Panel
items: Select "Tools"
Select "Email Deliverability"
Wait for the deliverability status
Escalate to Config & Analyse if result is "Invalid"

questions.text: If a spam test at mail-tester.com does not provide a score (the boat keeps rowing), what does this mean?
options: The email never reached the test server
The email is valid but delayed

questions.text: Which hosting package provides the largest email capacity?
What is the process for creating a email address on a domain ?
items: Confirm if there are email address spaces available
Select “Create”
Insert the customer’s desired email name (without domain)
Select “Generate”
Select “Create” again

questions.text: A customer wants to add a new email, but their package does not have capacity for any more email addresses to be added . What should you do?
questions.adminNotes: escalate to sales_specialist

questions.text: Which protocol synchronizes emails between server and device (read/deleted/replied)?
Outgoing mail is always sent using __________ protocol.
questions.adminNotes: SMTP

questions.text: How is a password reset performed ?
items: Locate the domain
Locate the email adress
Select “Manage” next to the email
Select “Generate” to create a new password
Select “Update Email Settings”

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they can receive emails but cannot send emails out. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customer is using outlook
  • The domain is hosted by Herotel
  • The device does have a active internet connection.
1. What is the next thing that needs to be confirmed in order to resolve the problem , provide a reason for your answer ?
questions.adminNotes:
1.SMTP server config to be confirmed
1. The customer can send indicating the domain is not suspended or blocked there for the server settings could be incorrect as only the outgoing is not working.

questions.text: Why is SSL/TLS recommended for email connections?
options: It encrypts communication with the mail server

questions.text: What are the risks of marking spam as “Not Spam”?
When is it safe to mark an email as “Not Spam”?
options: When it is from a trusted sender
When it is from a legitimate, expected source
When it is from an unknown sender
When you subscribed to the newsletter

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they have a new laptop and require assistance with configuring the email address on outlook.

1. What information is required by the mail program in order to configure the account ?

Scenario : You have just configured a email address on a laptop using the email program outlook.

1. How do you confirm if the configuration was succesfull ?
questions.adminNotes: Do a send & receive test

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they cannot send and receive. The following steps have been performed :
  • It has been confirmed that the device has internet connection
  • Any desk was used to verify the customers email server settings , it was confirmed the email is hosted by telkom.
1. What steps were missed that could have made the process more efficient and would have allowed the agent to confirm who hosts it before troubleshooting steps were performed ?
questions.adminNotes:
1. Confirming who host the domain by using the herotel portal 
1. Using the third party website to confirm who hosts the domain

questions.text: Provide examples of common third party domain providers :
questions.adminNotes:
1. Gmail
1. Telkom
1. Mweb
1. Yahoo

questions.text: What is email forwarding?
options: Deleting emails automatically after they are read
Moving emails to a spam folder
Sending all emails received on one address to another address
Changing the password of an account

questions.text: What must be done if you want to change an existing forwarding rule?
options: Do nothing — the system updates it automatically
Configure a new forwarder with the updated address

questions.text: Why would you change a customer’s email password?
options: If the password is forgotten or not working

questions.text: What is the purpose of a quota check?
options: To count the number of emails in the inbox
To confirm how much storage space is used vs available

questions.text: If a domain or email account has no storage space available, what should you do?
options: Educate the customer about deleting old emails
Escalate to Sales_specialist for a package upgrade

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they cannot sent / receive. The following was confirmed :
  • The customers domain is hosted by Herotel
  • The domain is not suspended
  • The qouta and disk used for the domain is not the same , there is still 5 gb of storage available on the domain
  • The qouta and disk used for the specific email address expereincing the problem is the same total.
  • The agent increased the email storage from 2 Gb to 3 Gb

1. Why did increasing the email storage resolve the problem ?
questions.adminNotes: The customer had no storage available on that email adress but still on the domain there for increasing it allowed the emails that are sent / received on that email address to continue storing on the server

questions.text: Which application is used to access the Herotel email portal ?
questions.adminNotes: WireGaurd

questions.text: What is the purpose of using Mail-tester.com?
options: To test the health of an email domain or address

questions.text: What does a 550 error often indicate?
options: Invalid or non-existent recipient email address

questions.text: A 5.4.1 error often means:
options: Anti-spam or security filters are blocking the email

questions.text: Which of the following can cause “Email server cannot be reached” errors in Outlook?
A password error in Outlook may mean:
options: The antivirus is out of date
The email account is blocked or the password is incorrect

answers.2: On chip it will be indicated on the top banner as well as on the teams outage channel to check any new outages
answers.3: If the Ap is offline and unreachable the ticket needs to be escalated to config as an offline and unreachable AP will affect multiple clients
answers.10:
The cables are plugged in incorrectly
The PoE does not have power
Possible network outage
The antenna reset or is damaged
answers.12: There is no active data for the customer, When searching for an active data session no results show up this could indicate that the clients pppoe is not authenticating on radius

rows: The Backhaul is offline
The NAS ran out of backup power during loadshedding
There was a break in by the tower and equipment was stolen
A upstream provider has a cable break.
questions.text: Match the scenario to the correct outage type.
What is step 2 in troubleshooting a wireless no internet qeury ?
options: Confirm power to the clients CPE ODU / Router
Confirm that there is no outage / maintanace

questions.text: Where can it be confirmed if a customer is affected by an outage ?
questions.adminNotes:
1. CHIP
1. 7.4.1 Network outages Internal SOU / NOR
1. 7.4.2 Network outages external NOR / SOU
1. 7.4.0 Planned maintanance

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they do not have internet access. The following has been confirmed :
  • The cables are correctly plugged in and the CPE is powered on
  • The AP the customer connects to shows offline on preseem
  • A ping test confirms the AP is unreachable.
1. What is the next step in order to resolve the problem
questions.adminNotes:
1. Update the ticket with all findings
1. Escalate to C & A

questions.text: What power supply is being shown in this image ?
What power supply is being shown in this image ?
Take a look at the image , state which port is responsible for which function and where the cables plugged into that port must lead.

pairs.left: data port , its connected to the router
black kettle cord , connected to a power source
the cable connection between the ODU and the antenna , it supplies the antenna with data and power

pairs.left: black kettle cord , must be connected to a power supply
The port that gives power to the antenna , the STP should connect here to the antenna
LAN port , UTP should be connected to the router
power indicator , indicating the unit is powered on

questions.text: Match the description to the correct section on the diagram describing a PoE splitter.
Provide 4 reasons why the ODU could be disconnected from the access point.
questions.adminNotes:
1. Possible network outage
1. POE does nto have power

questions.text: What tools are you going to use to troubleshoot a wireless no internet problem ?

Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following :
  • The cables are correctly plugged in and the equipment is powered on
  • The image indicating the status of the ADS (active data session)
1. What is the current status of the customers ADS and provide a reason for your answer
questions.adminNotes:
1. No ADS
1. There are not entries showing for the customers PPPoE username confirming there is not ADS

questions.text: Select the commonly used routers for a wireless service instilation.

answers.2:
Listen and understand the problem 
and own the customer (assure them that you'll help them every step of the way)
answers.5: you should confirm the physical setup of the wifi equipment to ensure correct steps are followed for that setup and the customer should be within 2 meters of the wifi router when doing the speed test
answers.10: the graph is indicating a download and upload speed of a little over 100 mbps ,yes it aligns with a typical speed test run as the upload test starts as the download test ends
answers.11: because the download test occurs during the first 3 minutes and the upload test takes place for the next 3 minutes once the download test is done
answers.13: the minimum parallel connection should be set to 2 and the minimum and max test duration should be set to 180
answers.15: yes it is valid because all the information for the speed tests in black text and not grayed out text
answers.17: rebooting the router allows all changes to be set properly and take affect
answers.19: auto channel should be set to true and the ssid for 5gh and 2.4gh should be changed to better identify them
answers.23: if the customers light level is above -27you need to navigate to the pon to check if its the only epon/ONT affected by high light levels if it is the ticket gets escalated to field opps if not it gets escalated to config and analyze
answers.25: there are multiple customers with light levels above -27 so the ticket should be escalated to config and analyze
answers.26: Due to the Transfer rate and other calculating factors speed tests can show be a few megabytes less than the package shows
answers.29: this provides you with an opportunity to educate the customer on how wifi and bandwidth works.This also equips the customer with the knowledge to test their internet speeds as well as do basic troubleshooting before contacting support this will help resolve any issues that arise faster and more efficiently

questions.text: What is the recommended way to greet a customer when answering a call?
options: Hello, Herotel technical, how can i assist?
Good Morning, Herotel, how can i help?
Thank you for calling, Herotel Technical Support, [Agent Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?
Herotel, [Agent Name] speaking. to whom am i speaking?

questions.text: Why is it important to smile while speaking to a customer over the phone?
options: It makes your voice sound more friendly and enthusiastic.

questions.text: List two ways you can show empathy to a customer during a call.
questions.adminNotes:
• Use phrases like “I understand how frustrating that must be.”
  • Actively listen and avoid interrupting.

questions.text: Why is it important to ask a client to test using a LAN cable?
options: It helps determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-related or affects all mediums.
LAN tests are required for documentation

questions.text: If a customer does not have band steering enabled, what should they test?
What physical setup should you confirm with the customer to rule out interference?
questions.adminNotes:
• Confirm the client has clear line of sight to the router.
  • Ensure the router is not placed on top of electronics.

questions.text: Which monitoring tool is used to view real-time throughput?
Why should the metrics view be set to “Last 15 minutes”?
options: To see average usage for the month over a 15 minute interval
To compare it to previous days usage

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • A speed test shows available bandwidth, not total capacity.

questions.text: If a speed test shows 50 Mbps but Preseem shows 100 Mbps during the same period, what does it indicate?
options: Another device on the network is using bandwidth

questions.text: Question: What do you observe in this graph? Does it align with a typical speed test run?
questions.adminNotes:
• Identify if throughput spikes during speed test period.
  • Confirm if it matches expected package speeds.

questions.text: Explain why the upload appears later than the download in the Preseem metrics graph while doing a speed test.
questions.adminNotes: • The test runs download first, then upload — so Preseem will reflect upload slightly later.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct speed test link?
What setting must be changed in the speed test to run a 6-minute test?
questions.adminNotes:
• Set parallel connections to minimum 2
  • Set test duration to 180 seconds for both min and max

questions.text: What happens after the download portion of the 6-minute test finishes?
Question: Based on this result, is the test complete and valid? How can you tell?
questions.adminNotes:
• All speed test results are no longer greyed out.
  • Download and upload values are shown clearly.

questions.text: What setting should be enabled on the router for optimal channel selection?
Why is it beneficial to reboot the router after enabling Auto Channel?
questions.adminNotes: • It allows the router to pick the best channel based on current interference when it reboots.

questions.text: Which wireless standard operates on the 5 GHz band?
Question: What change should be made here to improve performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Auto Channel is set to “False.”
  • Should change to “True” and commit the change.

questions.text: After changing the Auto Channel setting to “True,” what must you do next?
What is the acceptable light level range for fibre connections?
Where do you check a customer’s fibre light levels?
What action must be taken if a customer’s light level is -29 dBm?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Field Operations immediately.

questions.text: If multiple clients on the same OLT have bad light levels, where do you escalate the issue?
Question: What does this indicate and where should this be escalated?
questions.adminNotes:
• Multiple bad light levels = area issue.
  • Escalate to Config and Analyze.

questions.text: Explain why a speed test may not always reflect the customer's full package speed.
questions.adminNotes: • Because the test only shows what bandwidth is available at the time — other devices may be consuming bandwidth.

questions.text: If Preseem shows full throughput, but the customer says they have no devices connected, what could be the reason?
options: A hidden device may be using bandwidth
Fibre is too fast for Preseem to measure

questions.text: What is the last step in the troubleshooting flow if the issue is unresolved after all checks?
options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

answers.2: CHIP, teams, Preseem,Streamline
answers.3: Confirm if the outage affects one customer or multiple. If one client- field ops. If multiple- config and analyse
answers.10: cables plugged in incorrectly, possible network outage, PoE does not have power, antenna has reset or is damaged.
answers.12: There is no active PPPOE session. The PPPOE is not authenticating on the radius server

Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following :
  • The cables are correctly plugged in and the equipment is powered on

1. What is the current status of the customers ADS and provide a reason for your answer
questions.adminNotes:

questions.text: Select the commonly used routers for a wireless service instilation.

Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they are expereincing slow speed , you have confirmed the following :
  • The customer confirms that when the wireless service was installed a few years ago there were no trees in front of the direction where the antenna is pointing however in the last few months he has noticed that the trees do now seem to be at the same height as his ODU. 
  • It has also been confirmed that the speed is slow in general its not just specific times
Identify the possible cause of the problem
questions.adminNotes: • LOS is possibly obstructed between the ODU & AP / PTMP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they are in the process of downloading a file from one drive. How ever the file is 1 Gb big and only downloading at 5 Mbps. The customer has a 10 Mbps download by 5 Mbps upload package. You log into the customers antenna and confirm that the RX traffic shows 10 Mbps. What does this indicate.
questions.adminNotes: • The full download bandwidth is being utilized

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they would like to extend there network connection to a work shop on the same premises. The following has been confirmed :
  • The work shop is 300 m from the original point of instalstion 
  • You have confirmed that a ethernet cable starts degrading packets at 70 m +-
What solution can be offered to the customer to resolve this problem
questions.adminNotes: • Suggest a PTP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer brings a damaged router into the office stating it has been hit by lighting. The customer does not wish to accumalate any aditional costs and supplies a wireless extender with 2 ariels into the office to be reconfigured as the Gateway. Why will this not resolve the problem.
options: The extender cannot support the customers bandwidth
The extender cannot be configured in Gateway mode
The extender was not supplied by Herotel
The extenders make & model is not well known

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer would like to extend his Wi-Fi connectivity to his lapa by his swimming pool. The following has been confirmed :
  • The lapa is 50 m from the original instilation point
  • The connecting the lapa's equipment to the house's equipment will be exposed to weather
What is the best cabling solution for the customers request
questions.adminNotes: • STP

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following : 
  • The customer has a fiber package
  • The ONT / ONU seems to not have power as the customer states there are no lights burning on it
  • The Gateway how ever does have lights burning and there is a ethernet connection present between the ONT / ONU & the Gateway
  • There is no active data session for the radius user
What is the cause of the customer not having a active data session and why is this your answer.
questions.adminNotes:
• ONT / ONU does not have power
  • There is nothing to convert the fibre signal received from the herotel network into a copper signal so that the gateway can comunicate to and from the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in and states that recently they have been doing some construction of a building in the back of their yard. They removed a pole which connected multiple fiber customers to the Herotel network.
  • Why did removing the pole disconnect multiple customers ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The pole has a dome which connects the drop cables into the customers premises
  • The dome also connects the customers to the rest of the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacted in and confirms that there internet feels slow. When they google or try to upload something to one drive it fails and multiple attempts needs to be made before it uploads. You have confirmed the following 
  • The customer is using UTP cabling on the premises which is a office
  • All power sources for the offices originate from one point to keep the cabling neat
  • The UTP cable that connects the access point to the switch runs over the power cabling
What is the possible cause of the problems being expereinced by the customer and why do you suspect this is the cause of the problem.
questions.adminNotes:
• EMI
  • EMI is caused by high currents

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer who has 5 flats in back of his yard contacts in stating that his renters have reported slow speed & intermitted connectivity when they are connected to the Wi-Fi. You have confirmed the following :
  • Each flat has there own access point with its own SSID credentials
  • Each access point is connected via a ethernet cable to a switch in the customers home
  • The flats are 10 m apart & only one story
What could be the possible cause of the slow speed / intermitted connectivty.
questions.adminNotes: • Wireless interference

questions.text:
Scenario : A customers contacts into the First Line Support department regarding slow speed & intermitted connectivty. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customers ethernet cable between the ODU & PoE is syncing at 10 Mbps
  • The transmision method is described by the attached image
Why is this transmission method not optimal ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Half-Duplex
  • The device cannot sent & receive at the time which causes the network to feel slow

questions.text:
Scenario : A cutomer contacts in and states that they would like to configure port forwarding for their camera's that they can view them remotely. You have confirmed the following :
  • A wireless install is present on the premises
  • The ODU is configured in bridge mode
Where should the port forwarding rules be configured and why ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Gateway
  • The ODU is in bridge mode meaning the packets from the ODU pass on to the gateway , the gateway performs the natting

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customers ONU status is wire down
  • Multiple customers are confirmed wire down on the same EPON at the same time
Which department should this problem be escalated to ?

Scenario : A customer contacts in and states they would like an additional installation on another premises. 
Which department can assist with the customers query.

Which program can the following functions be performed on :
  • Ticket Creation
  • Communication between a customer and a agent
  • Communication transfer between the customer and another department
  • Ticket escalation to another department

rows: Confirming the PPPoE username & PPPoE password
Changing the Wi-Fi SSID / Password

No the agent did not follow the proper protocol. The agent was supposed to have made 4 attempts to the customer at intervals of 2 hours within the shift. If the intervals fall over to the next shift then perform a shift handover procedure(Team leader to assist) to the incident. The correct procedure would be to attempt to reach the customer every 2 hours and on every attempt log it on the Qcontact stating the number dialed time and date of the call. The is also a email template provided for the Herotel technical support agents to notify customer of the attempts that have been made to contact them. 
Finally a closure template would then be implemented to inform the user of the number of ttempts have been made on which number when and how. It would again encourage the customer to call in for assistance to the pertaining incident.

answers.0: No, the agent didn't follow the correct steps. After attempting to call the customer again, the agent should have logged the attempt(s) made and sent an email stating that a call was made, what number he dialed, the time and date of the call and request for the customer to specify an alternative number or a better time to call. After that, the agent should have attempted to call the customer again every 2 hours within his shift and after each call attempt, send an email following the same structure as the initial email. Finally, after 4 call attempts with no response, the agent should have sent a final email notifying the client that multiple call attempts have been made, the ticket will now be closed and the client may reach out again should assistance still be required.

answers.2:
You can show empathy and build rapport with the customer during a call by:
1. Listening attentively to their problem and acknowledging it.
2. Apologising for the issue and assuring them that you're going to help.
answers.10: This graph tells us that the customer's usage tends to spike around 12 and they intermittently use the max bandwidth that their package allows.
answers.11: You should ask the customer to run a speed test before analysing throughput.
answers.13: We ask the customer to reboot the router after enabling auto channel so that the router can apply the new change and it can take effect.
answers.15: "Auto channel enabled" needs to be changed from false to true on both bands so that the router can automatically choose the best channel for performance.
answers.20: This tells us that the sector is experiencing latency spikes and customers connected that sector will experience slow speeds as a result.
answers.22: If both the customer and the sector show unstable latency, the issue should be escalated to config and analyse.
answers.24: If all tests are complete and no faults are found, I then need to update the ticket accordingly and close it after documenting all troubleshooting steps taken to reach the conclusion.
answers.26: From this image I can conclude that both the health and RF scores are poor and the issue needs to be escalated to config and analyse as a result.

questions.text: What is the recommended way to greet a customer on a support call?
options: Thank you for calling, this is [Name].
Thank you for calling Herotel Technical Support, [Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?
Hey, what seems to be the problem?

questions.text: What is the purpose of smiling while speaking on a call?
options: It makes your voice sound friendly and enthusiastic

questions.text: List two ways to show empathy and build rapport with the customer during a call.
questions.adminNotes:
• “I understand this must be frustrating.”
  • Use active listening and repeat their issue back.
  • Friendly tone, no sighs or frustration.

questions.text: Why do we ask the customer to test using a LAN cable?
options: Speed tests only works on LAN
To determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-specific or across all mediums

questions.text: If the customer does not have band steering enabled, which frequencies must they test?
What two physical factors around the router could affect wireless performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Router location: not behind objects, in cupboards, or stacked.
  • Avoid interference from TVs, microwaves, and fish tanks.

questions.text: Which tool is used to monitor real-time wireless throughput?
Why should we change the metrics view from 2 weeks to 15 minutes in Preseem?
options: To better match the speed test timeframe
It shows the average across all users

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • Available bandwidth at a specific moment — not the full capacity of the package.

questions.text: In Preseem, what does it mean if the yellow line meets the green line?
options: The client is experiencing packet loss
The client is maxing out their plan

questions.text: Question: What does this graph tell you about the customer’s usage?
questions.adminNotes: • Customer is using full package speed — no fault on the ISP side.

questions.text: What should you ask the customer to do before analysing throughput?
questions.adminNotes: • Ask the customer to run 3 consecutive speed tests and inform you of the times.

questions.text: What Wi-Fi setting allows the router to automatically choose the best frequency band?
Why should we ask the customer to reboot the router after enabling auto channel?
questions.adminNotes: • To apply the new wireless settings and ensure the router reselects optimal channels.

questions.text: Which wireless standard applies to the 5 GHz band?
Question: What setting needs to be changed here and why?
What must be done after changing a router setting on the ACS page?
What does good RF and good health indicate?
What should you do if RF is good but health score is bad?
What do bad RF and bad health score indicate?
options: the frequency needs to be changed to make it better
Problem between AP and client — escalate to config for further analysis.
the client needs to move the ODU to make it better

questions.text: Question: What does this tell you about the sector?
questions.adminNotes: • Sector experiencing performance issues — possibly oversubscribed or congested.

questions.text: If latency is only spiking on the client and not the sector, who do you escalate to?
options: Config and Analyze to change the clients frequency
Field Operations to check for interference or alignment problems.
Area manager to call the client and calm him down

questions.text: What should you do if both the customer and sector show unstable latency?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Config and Analyze to check channel width, power, and access point settings.

questions.text: If speed test from the sector to client is below package speed, what must be done?
When all tests are complete and no fault is found, what is your next step?
questions.adminNotes: • Educate the customer on Wi-Fi limitations, device usage, and recommend testing on LAN.

questions.text: What’s the main benefit of reviewing latency graphs on both customer and sector?
options: It tells you what equipment they have
It identifies where the fault lies

questions.text: Question: What should you conclude from this and what action is required?
questions.adminNotes: • Indicates poor signal and stability — escalate to config and possibly field ops.

answers.0: The possible problem is an issue on the A, the next step is to check the other customers connected at the OLT and if there are multiple users, escalate to the config and analyze and if it only one user escalate to the field ops.
answers.1: The metric indicates that the customer account is maximizing the full bandwidth, I would educate the customer by letting them know that if there are a lot of devices connected to the network it would generally be slow also based on their plan, they should consider increasing the plan seamless connection.
answers.2:
The metrics indicate that the user customer is maximizing their full bandwidth, the blue line is not able which might indicate a possible problem on the AP. 
Latency graph shows how the user connection is, what bandwidth are they using and if they are maximizing their connection.
answers.3: Both the RF and Health score are bad, the next step will be to escalate to the config and analyse team.
answers.4: Further steps will be to run our own speed test up to 180 seconds on fast.com to confirm the speed test results, another thing would be that the customers TV is not compatible with using the maximum package the user has so they should get it checked out.
answers.5: False, the speed test should take 180 seconds for accurate results.
answers.6: I would explain to the user that the results on preseem will be different if there were other devices also connected on the network when they were running the speed test, for accurate results the user should disconnect all the other devices and only leave the one they are testing with.
answers.9: It is the wireless one, the blue line indicates whether there is an issue on the AP/ODU or not.
answers.10: The graph indicates that the customers equipment might be out of spec or the ODU might be damaged.
answers.11: It indicates that the users is maxing their download speed and not so much on the upload speed as it is less than that of their package.
answers.12: The possible problem might be originating from the customers ODU as the AP seems to be healthy.

answers.2:
1. Listen to their problem and acknowledge their frustration/situation at hand.
2. Apologise and reassure them that you've heard their issue and you're going to help.
answers.5:
1. I should confirm that the customer is near the router (no further than 2M)
2. I should confirm that the customer is connected to the router Via LAN cable.
answers.10: The download peaks first followed by the upload that peaks shortly after, which aligns with a typical speed test run.
answers.11: This happens because the speed test runs the download first and only starts on the upload once the download is complete.
answers.13: The "test duration" setting needs to be changed to run a 6-minute test.
answers.15: Based on the result I can tell the the test is not complete and valid. This is because the "settings" tab has not been clicked and the "parallel connections" aswell as "Test duaration" could not have been set to the correct values if the settings was not opened.
answers.17: It's important to reboot the router so that the new settings can be applied and take effect.
answers.19: "Auto channel enabled" on the the 2.4GHz band should be changed from "false" to "true" and then the change needs to be committed in order to take effect.
answers.23: If a customer's light level is -29 dBm you need to find out whether the issue is affecting only that customer or multiple customers. If it's affecting only that customer, the ticket needs to be updated with the customer's problem and troubleshooting steps taken then escalated to field operations, and if it's affecting multiple customers, the ticket needs to be updated with the customers problem and troubleshooting steps taken, and then escalated to config and analyse.
answers.25: This indicates that more than one customer's network is experiencing bad fibre light levels and it should therefore be escalated to config and analyse.
answers.26: Speed tests might not always reflect the customer's full package speed due to transfer rates and other calculating factors like a customer using a device that only supports 2.4GHz.
answers.29:
It's important to guide the customer through each test so that:
1. You can receive real-time and accurate results.
2. You can empower the customer with knowledge on how to perform these steps in future.
3. You can accurately determine whether the issue is with the customers internal network or Herotel's network.

options: A hidden device may be using bandwidth
Fibre is too fast for Preseem to measure

questions.text: What is the last step in the troubleshooting flow if the issue is unresolved after all checks?
options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

answers.1: To build trust and reassure the customer that their issues are being heard.
answers.2:
1. Check the account status
2. Check the outage status 
3. Check if there is any active ticket for the issue
answers.3: Inform the customer that there is an active network outage affecting them and our technical support team is onsite fixing the issue and they will receive communication when the issue has been fixed.
answers.4: Create a ticket with all the screenshots of the issues and escalate to the field ops.
answers.5:
1. Check if they have an active job card if not create a ticket.
2. Confirm if is affecting one customer or multiple, if single customer escalate to field ops and if there are multiple customers escalate to config and analyze.
answers.7: The cause of the problem is that the STP and Ethernet cable are plugged incorrectly, the STP goes into the LAN and the antenna goes into the gateway.
answers.8:
1. Ping the IP address of the AP to check the communication.
2. Check if the information is being polled from the AP on preseem.
answers.11: The ethernet cable is connected to the wrong port.
answers.14: ONU, it is used to convert fibre signals into copper signals.
answers.16: The router has reset to default.
answers.17: The customer wifi is not not on, the customer is not connected to any wifi.

questions.adminNotes:

  • Confirm if the customer is affected by a outage

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they have multiple instilations. You have confirmed that the customers outage status is active and red. 
  • What is the next step.

A customer contacts in and states that they do no have internet access. You have confirmed the following :

  • The ethernet cables are connected correctly

What is the next step to aid the customer in resolving the problem
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate the ticket to FO

questions.text:

  • The equipment is powered on and has lights
  • There is a red light flashing on and off on the ONT 
  • The customers ONT status according to preseem is wire down
What is the next step ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Confirm if its a singular customer affected on the PON
  • Escalate to FO if singular , escalate to C & A if multiple (same time & same PON)

Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that there internet connection has been offline for a few days. You have confirmed the following :

  • The customer has a wireless service 
  • The stp cable from the antenna is plugged in by the LAN port on the PoE and the ethernet cable leading to the gateway is plugged in by the ethernet port

What is the cause of the problem the customer is expereincing ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The ethernet cables are incorrect , the stp should be in the poe port and the cable to the gateway should be in the LAN port
  • The customers ODU is not getting power

questions.text: Scenario :  A customer contacts in and sttes that they have a red light flasing on and off by the LOS light on there ONT. You confirm the status on preseem is as the attached image. What does this indicate ?
options: The customers equipment is not getting power
The equipment is not getting fiber light
The customers drop cable could be damaged

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that there internet connected is currently offline. The following steps have been performed :
  • Account is active Outage status is normal
  • The CPE is powered on as there are lights on all the equipment The SSID is showing Herotel 
  • The pppoe credentials show provisioning on ACS

What does the above completed steps indicate ?
questions.adminNotes: • The customers router reset to default settings

questions.text: Scenario : You are in the process of assisting a customer via whatsapp that is stating that when the browse on there laptop it states this site cannot be reached. During troubleshooting the customer sends you the attached screen shot. What is the cause of the problem ?
questions.adminNotes: • The customers Wi-Fi is disabled.

answers.2: THE OLT IS A DEVICE WHERE THE MULTIPLE EPONS IN AN AREA CONNECT TO HENCE IT CANNOT BE ON A CUSTOMERS PREMISE
answers.3:
GREET PROFESSIONALLY.
LISTEN & UNDERSTAND.
KNOW WHAT TO DO & DO.
OWN THE CUSTOMER.
answers.5: AS AN AGENT , I WOULD HAVE TO INFORM THE CUSTOMER THAT THERE IS AN ACTIVE OUTAGE IN THEIR AREA AND A TICKET CANNOT BE CREATED AS OUR FIELD OPERATIONS TEAM IS ALREADY AWARE AND DEALING WITH THE ISSSUE AFFECTING THEM, I WILL ALSO LET THE USER KNOW THAT THEY WILL RECEIVE COMMUNICATION EVERY 2 HOURS WITH AN UPDATE.
answers.8: A WIRE DOWN RELATES AND REFERS TO THE PHYSICAL BREAK IN THE FIBRE CABLES HENCE FIELD OPERATIONS WILL BE THE RIGHT TEAM.
answers.9: BECAUSE ALL EQUIPMENT AND CABLES ARE THE ONES THAT PROVIDE THE SERVICE TO THE USER, IF A ONE IS SHORT THEN THE OTHER CANNOT FUNCTION OR PROVIDE INTERNET CONNECTIVITY.
answers.10: A CALIX HAS A BUILT IN SPLICE CASSETTE AND COMMONLY USED RIUTERS HAS A SPICE CASSETTE CONNECTED TO IT.
11: The drop cable is spliced into the dome & the splice casette on the customer premises
One end of the patch lead is plugged into the splice casette
The other end of the patch lead is plugged into the ONU
one end of the LAN cable is plugged into the ONU
The ONU's power supply needs to be plugged in and on by a wall socket
The other end of the LAN cable is plugged into the WAN / Internet port on the Gateway
Other ports not dedicated as WAN / Internet can be used to connect devices such as Tv's , Concoles ect.
12: The drop cable is spliced into the dome & the splice casette on the customer premises
One end of the patch lead is plugged into the splice casette
The other end of the patch lead is plugged into the PON port on the Gateway
The LAN ports on the Gateway are to connect other devices such as TV's , computers ect.
answers.16: BLANK
answers.20: FOR A SINGLE DEVICE, CUSTOMER WILL BE ADVISED THAT THE ISSUE APPEARS TO BE ON THE DEVICE AND THEY SHOULD GET IT CHECKED OUT.
answers.22: BECAUSE A profile CAN ONLY BE FOUND VIA A WAN MAC IF THE ROUTER HAS RESET, IT CANNOT BE FOUND USING THE OTHER METHODS BECAUSE IT NEEDS TO BE CONFIGURED again.
answers.24: NO, ANYTHING OTHER THAN LAST 15 MINUTES IT MEANS THAT THE ROUTER IS OFFLINE AND CANNOT BE REACHED.
answers.28: BECAUSE THE EPON STRING ALLOWS US TO BE ABLE TO SEE THE OVERVIEW OF THE CUSTOMERS ONT.

questions.adminNotes: • No , the status needs to be last 15 minutes other wise the router is not reachable and changes on it is not possible

questions.text: What tools can be used to confirm if the customers CPE is connected to the Herotel network ?
Scenario : You have identified that multiple customers on the same OLT and EPON are wire down. According to Preseem the customers disconnected at the same time , what would be your next step.
options: Update the ticket with all findings and escalate to Config & Analyse

questions.text: How would you locate a customers ONU on the OLT ?
options: Search using the customer account code
Search using the customers data account
Search using the customers EPON string
Search using the customers MAC address

questions.text: Explain the reason for your answer in question 29
questions.adminNotes: • The EPON string is located on preseem and this method is also trained

answers.2: The OLT is located at Herotel's exchange and not the customers premises. The OLT is the central device where all the PONs in the area connect to, it manages communication with multiple ONTs and therefore cannot be located on the customers premises.
answers.3:
The three key points are:
1.Greeting the customer professionally
2.Listen to and understand the customers problem (empathy)
3.Own the customer
answers.5:
If any objections take place from the customer after informing them that they're part of a network outage, I should:
.Acknowledge the customers frustration and show a level of understanding
.Notify the customer that they will be receiving real time updates via whatsapp, keeping them up to par on any progress that's been made.
answers.8: Wire down could be an indication of faulty equipment
answers.9: It's important because if cables are not correctly connected or equipment is not powered on, the internet service will not function properly resulting in the customer experiencing slow speeds/latency variation or no internet at all.
answers.10: A commonly used router installation has a separate ONT/ONU unit that connects to the router, whereas an XPON/Calix installation has an ONT/ONU integrated into the router.
11: The drop cable is spliced into the dome & the splice casette on the customer premises
One end of the patch lead is plugged into the splice casette
The other end of the patch lead is plugged into the ONU
The ONU's power supply needs to be plugged in and on by a wall socket
one end of the LAN cable is plugged into the ONU
The other end of the LAN cable is plugged into the WAN / Internet port on the Gateway
Other ports not dedicated as WAN / Internet can be used to connect devices such as Tv's , Concoles ect.
12: The drop cable is spliced into the dome & the splice casette on the customer premises
One end of the patch lead is plugged into the splice casette
The other end of the patch lead is plugged into the PON port on the Gateway
The LAN ports on the Gateway are to connect other devices such as TV's , computers ect.
answers.16: blank clolumn
answers.20: If only one device is showcasing issues, it's usually not an issue with the herotel equipment and network itself and is most probably a device issue. Instead of escalating you should inform the customer that the device might be the issue and that they should get it checked out.
answers.22: A reset router cannot be searched using the PPPoE username because it would've reset back to default meaning the PPPoE that was assigned before the reset will be invalid. In-order to search for a router using WAN IP would require the router to have an active session which is not possible after it's been reset as it will not be able to authenticate any longer. This means the only two methods that can be used to search for a router is the serial number (doesn't change) and the WANMAC address method.
answers.24: No this router is not reachable on the herotel network. An indication that it is reachable would be last 15 minutes. Anything more than last 15 minutes show that the router is most probably offline cause info gets polled every 15 minutes.
answers.28: The EPON sting is used to identify which PON a customers ONU is connected to which will help you locate the customers router

answers.1: Showing empathy assures the customer that their issues are heard and that the agent is listening and understand their frustration.
answers.2: The first step is to confirm if the user has an active outage status or not and also check if their account is active or suspended.
answers.3: The next step is to inform the user that there is an active network outage that the team is currently addressing and a ticket will not be necessary as the team will update once everything is fixed.
answers.4: The next step will be to ask the user to reboot if they have not done so, and to ask confirm if with can see the customers equipment on our network.
answers.5: The next step will be to check if there is an active work order for the customer and if not create a ticket detailing all the findings and escalate to the field ops team.
answers.7: The cause of the problem is that the stp cable and the ethernet cable are plugged in incorrectly, the stp goes into the LAN and the antenna plugs into gateway.
answers.8: The next step is to check the other users connected to the AP, to be able to see if it is only affecting one user (escalate to field ops) or multiple users escalate to (config and analyse)
answers.11: The cause of the problem is that the ethernet cable is connected to the wrong port.
answers.14: It is a PoE, it connects the ports connected to the indoor system into the home system.
answers.16: It indicates that the router has reset to default.
answers.17: The customer is not connected to the WI-FI (the WI-FI is off) and also not connected via the LAN cable.

answers.0:
No, the customer did not follow all the call back procedure steps. The correct procedure is:
1. Initial call attempt - call the customer and if they do not answer, log the attempt and send them an email stating that a call was made along with with the number dialed, the date and time and request for the customer to specify an alternative number for the call and also a better time for the callback.
2. Subsequent call attempts - attempt the call every 2 hours in an 8 hours shifts (4 call attempts) and after each unsuccessful call, log the attempt and send the user an email.
3. Final attempt and closure - after the 4 call attempts and there is still no response, send a final email notifying the customer that multiple attempts were made to reach them, and the ticket will be closed due to no response and they may reach out again should they still require assistance. 
It is also important to documents all calls and emails sent by updating the ticket.

answers.2:
1. Shielded Twisted Pair: Great for outside cables that might be exposed to oxidation, moisture and server weather. Heavy duty cabling. Outside in towers.
2. Unshielded Twisted Pair: This is commonly used for cables that emit light electric pulses, small and portable in most cases that is your ethernet cables. They are not made for severe weather conditions as they would tear off easily.
answers.5:
There needs to be an IP Pool, where a vast number of pool addresses are available to be leased when requested.
The lease time is important as it allows a device to use an Ip address for a certain period of time and not hog the address as to when it has an expired session it may then request again to be granted more time to use it.
The unique identifier (MAC Address) is used to know where the requested IP address is supposed to be sent to.
The DHCP server acts as a medium for this type of communication to occur.
answers.7: True. NAS is a device that connects to an access point through an ethernet cable, that allows it to supply internet access to multiple devices connected to that specific LAN with a single public address. Without using NAT scheme the address would be discarded by the internet as they would be classified as private addresses that are not meant for public usage. Therefore, the NAS does perform NAT.

questions.text: Scenario : A customer is requesting that the email address for her business sales team is configured on both her personal computer and the company computer. The customer would like to be able to view the emails in real time and confirm if her sales team is responding in a timely manner to the emails received. What email protocol would best suit this scenario ?

answers.1: Showing empathy towards a customer builds trust and rapport, it makes the customer feel comfortable and calm enough to communicate their issue and stay engaged right through the troubleshooting process.
answers.2: First we will guide the customer to check that all their equipment is plugged in, powered and connected correctly. Once that is done, we'll locate the customers profile on CHIP using their account code, after we've located the customers account, we will check their account status and outage status, we can also confirm if the customer is apart of an outage by checking one of the teams outage channel. If we confirm that the customer's equipment if functioning and powered correctly and that they're not suspended or apart of a confirmed network outage we can move on with the troubleshooting.
answers.3: We need to search the customers account code on the radius server to locate the customer's active data sessions, this will help us identify the exact installations that are apart of the network outage. Once we have this information, we should inform the customer about which installation(s) are currently experiencing the outage and notify them that they'll receive live status updates on Whatsapp relating to progress made on the outage if they disagree with or dispute the outage status.
answers.4: We need to open up Chip to see the customer's account and outage status to see if their equipment is able to communicate with the Herotel network.
answers.5: I now need to check the PON that the customer's ONT connects to, to see if more than one customer is affected by this wire down or if it's only that specific customer. If it's multiple customer's, I need to escalate it to config and analyse if it's only the one customer's ONT affected then I need to escalate it to field operations.
answers.7: The customer's radius user is not authenticating to the radius server therefore an active data session cannot be established, this could either be due to the customer being suspended or it can be due to a an access point being down.
answers.8: We need to ping the AP to see if the Herotel network is still reachable, this is because an "offline" status is not always an indication that the Herotel network is unreachable. If the Herotel network as a whole is unreachable, then the problem needs to be escalated to config and analyse, this would also explain why the customer's ODU shows an "offline status", any equipment connected to the offline AP with showcase as offline.
answers.11: The ethernet cable is connected to one of the LAN ports on the gateway instead of being connected to the WAN port.
answers.14: The equipment being shown in the picture is an ONT. The ONT is responsible for converting fibre light to ethernet signal that it sends to the gateway.
answers.16: The above steps indicate that the customers gateway has reset back to default configurations.
answers.17: The customer has the wifi setting on their laptop turned off. They need to turn it on in order for the laptop to discover their network so that they can connect to it.

comments.3: The outage status still needs to be confirmed on the outage channels or using the v2 chip

questions.text: Select the commonly used routers for a wireless service instilation.

answers.0: The possible cause of the problem is, the trees are blocking the line of sight between the customer's ODU and the AP.
answers.1: This indicates that the full download bandwidth is being used.
answers.2: A PTP solution can be offered to the customer to extend their network connection.
answers.4: STP would be the best cabling solution for the customers request.
answers.5: The cause is the ONT/ONU being off/having no power. This is because there is nothing to convert the fibre light into ethernet signal that the router can use, if the ONT is off.
answers.6: The pole likely had a dome on it that connects the customers to the Herotel network via a drop cable. If this pole is removed or the dome is damaged/removed, the customer's premises loses it's connection to the Herotel network therefore disconnecting them.
answers.7: The possible cause of the problem is electromagnetic interference. The customer is using a UTP cable meaning the twisted pair cables have little to no insulation protecting them from the eletromagnetic interference from the power cables that the UTP us running over.
answers.8: The possible cause of the slow speed/intermitted connectivity is wireless interference. The flats are in very proximity to one another which could be causing a signal clash.
answers.9: The transmission method described in the image is a Half duplex. This transmission method is not optimal because data cannot be sent and received by a device simultaneously in a half duplex set up, data can only either be sent or received by a device at a time which makes the network feel slow.
answers.10: The port forwarding rules should be configured on the gateway because the gateway receives the data packets and is responsible for sending the data packets to the correct device that's connected to it.
answers.17: I would locate the customer's router on ACS by using the router's MAC address.
answers.22: This error indicates that the radius user has been blacklisted/cannot attempt to authenticate for 5minutes and it's usually caused by 3 failed authentication attempts in a row which could be due to using incorrect or non-existent PPPoE details.
answers.24: The cause if this issue is that a Herotel business client is trying to authenticate to the normal Herotel radius server. Herotel business clients have their own radius server and they need to be proxied to that server inorder for the issue to be resolved.
answers.25: The details that can be used to confirm a session on the radius server are the PPPoE username (everything before the @) or the customer's account code. You use the username method when you want to confirm a session of a customer that has only one installation, if a customer has multiple installations, you use the account code method of searching.
answers.26: I would use the account code.
answers.29: The customer's domain is suspended, this is indicated by the red colored outer panel.
answers.30: The customer has utilised the full capacity that the email address allows. Quota is the amount of storage an email address has left and disk used is the amount of that storage that's already been used, if disk used=quota, the customer can no longer send and recieve.
answers.31: The possible cause of this issue is, the emails are not delivering, this is indicated by the invalid deliverability check. This is usually caused by some information not being correctly configured and this has to be escalated to config and analyse to be resolved.
answers.32: We need to see how many emails they have added and if their package allows for more to be added.
answers.34: No. The agent should call the customer back atleast once every two hours for the duration of their shift and they should send an email each time confirming that they're trying to reach the customer before closing the ticket and sending a final closure email.
answers.36: The ticket type should be changed, The "assigned to" needs to be changed and the department should be changed to "national_sales".

• EMI
  • EMI is caused by high currents

answers.0: The client is not receiving enough fibre light. escalate the issue to field operation
answers.1: The clients entire bandwidth is being utilized. Advice the client that they must connect less devices or upgrade to a higher package that will be able to sustain all the devices connected. should they want to be upgraded the matter must be escalated to sales.
answers.2: The metrics indicate that the problems lies on the AP. Latency is the speed information travels and respond between networks.
answers.3: The RF and health score are both red. the problem needs to be escalated to Config and Analyse.
answers.4: The customer needs to connect their TV via ethernet and confirm if it still buffers when connected via that. Should it not buffer than it means that the problem lies with wireless connection. As an agent also advice the client that the wireless connection could be disturbed because of the distance or even barriers like walls, and that could affect the connection
answers.5: False, a fibre connection will only populate after 2min because fibre has not gone inline like wireless that populate in 5 seconds.
answers.6: The reason why a speed test could show less is because another device could still be accessing or is connected to the network or background apps like whatsapp on the device that performed the speed test are running. However on preseem as the agent I will be able to see the correct speed at the time of the speed test.
answers.9: Service, the blue line indicates the latency
answers.10: The problem lies on the CPE's. Green and yellow line indicates the ODU
answers.11: The client does not over utilize their bandwidth, meaning that the client should not be expiriencing any problem with their line.
answers.12: Upstream providers. or confirm with the client how far away from the router they are?

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating they are expereincing slow speed. The following steps have been performed : 
  • The customer has a 50 Mbps symetrical fibre package
  • The speed test results does not match the customers package even via ethernet
  • The router configurations are correct
  • The customers fibre light is -36.5
1. What is the possible cause of the ploblem ?
2. What is the next step ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The customers fibre light is more then -27
  • Confirm if all customer connected to the OLT & PON have bad fibre light or only the singular customer

questions.text:
Scenario : A fibre customer contacts in and states that they have been expereincing slow speed for the past week.  The following has been confirmed :
  • There were no reported degraded service outage in the past week
  • The customer confirms there children are on school vacation
  • The customer package is a 20 Mbos download & 10 Mbps upload wireelss package
  • The customer performed a speed test and the attached preseem metrics are the results.
1. What does the metrics indicate ?
2. How would you educate the customer on the problem and its cause ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The customer is utilizing there full bandwidth that the package offers
  • Agent educates the customer on how bandwidth works

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that there connection feels slow and at times very intermitted. You have performed the following troubleshooting steps :
  • The customer is not part of a outage affecting the AP they are connecting to
  • The customers RF score is 4.07 & ok
  • The customers power for the equipment is stable 
  • The preseem metrics are as attached
1. What do the metrics indicate ?
2. What is latency ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The customers ODU , in home & AP has high latency indicating a problm on the AP
  • Latency is how long packets take from point a to reach point b

questions.text:
During the troubleshooting process you open the AP metrics (find the metrics attached).
  • What do the metrics indicate ?
  • What is the next recommended step ?
questions.adminNotes:
• There is a problem on the Ap as both the health & RF score is bad.
  • Escalate to C & A

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in stating that when they perform a speed test via there computer connected with a ethernet cable then the speed is correct. But when they watch tv which is connected via wi-fi it buffers. Speed tests performed via the Wi-Fi is also significantly lower then the ethernet test. Keep in mind the customer has a 100 Mbps symetrical fibre connection.
What further steps can be performed with the customer to narrow down if the problem is via the Wi-Fi or if the network in general could be expereincing the problem ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Distance from device to router
  • Freqeuncy to which the device is connected

questions.text:
True / False : A fibre customer can perform a speed test and 10 seconds later the speed test results will populate in the metrics. 
  • Provide a reason for your answer.
questions.adminNotes:
• False
  • Fibre customers poll every 2 minutes as they have not gone inline

questions.text:
Scenario : You are in the process of guiding the customer to perform a speed test. After the speed test the preseem metrics shows that the customer was utilizing the full available bandwidth in that period how ever the speed test results shows less.

questions.adminNotes: • key points mentioned : Speed test only shows whats available not what is being utilized

answers.29: To educate the customer in case they face the same issue again in the near future.
