It will also help in clear issues being reported so that as an agent you can assist and resolve the issue quicker to restore connectivity.

questions.text: What is the recommended way to greet a customer when answering a call?
options: Hello, Herotel technical, how can i assist?
Good Morning, Herotel, how can i help?
Thank you for calling, Herotel Technical Support, [Agent Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?
Herotel, [Agent Name] speaking. to whom am i speaking?

questions.text: Why is it important to smile while speaking to a customer over the phone?
options: It makes your voice sound more friendly and enthusiastic.

questions.text: List two ways you can show empathy to a customer during a call.
questions.adminNotes:
• Use phrases like “I understand how frustrating that must be.”
  • Actively listen and avoid interrupting.

questions.text: Why is it important to ask a client to test using a LAN cable?
options: It helps determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-related or affects all mediums.
LAN tests are required for documentation

questions.text: If a customer does not have band steering enabled, what should they test?
What physical setup should you confirm with the customer to rule out interference?
questions.adminNotes:
• Confirm the client has clear line of sight to the router.
  • Ensure the router is not placed on top of electronics.

questions.text: Which monitoring tool is used to view real-time throughput?
Why should the metrics view be set to “Last 15 minutes”?
options: To see average usage for the month over a 15 minute interval
To compare it to previous days usage

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • A speed test shows available bandwidth, not total capacity.

questions.text: If a speed test shows 50 Mbps but Preseem shows 100 Mbps during the same period, what does it indicate?
options: Another device on the network is using bandwidth

questions.text: Question: What do you observe in this graph? Does it align with a typical speed test run?
questions.adminNotes:
• Identify if throughput spikes during speed test period.
  • Confirm if it matches expected package speeds.

questions.text: Explain why the upload appears later than the download in the Preseem metrics graph while doing a speed test.
questions.adminNotes: • The test runs download first, then upload — so Preseem will reflect upload slightly later.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct speed test link?
What setting must be changed in the speed test to run a 6-minute test?
questions.adminNotes:
• Set parallel connections to minimum 2
  • Set test duration to 180 seconds for both min and max

questions.text: What happens after the download portion of the 6-minute test finishes?
Question: Based on this result, is the test complete and valid? How can you tell?
questions.adminNotes:
• All speed test results are no longer greyed out.
  • Download and upload values are shown clearly.

questions.text: What setting should be enabled on the router for optimal channel selection?
Why is it beneficial to reboot the router after enabling Auto Channel?
questions.adminNotes: • It allows the router to pick the best channel based on current interference when it reboots.

questions.text: Which wireless standard operates on the 5 GHz band?
Question: What change should be made here to improve performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Auto Channel is set to “False.”
  • Should change to “True” and commit the change.

questions.text: After changing the Auto Channel setting to “True,” what must you do next?
What is the acceptable light level range for fibre connections?
Where do you check a customer’s fibre light levels?
What action must be taken if a customer’s light level is -29 dBm?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Field Operations immediately.

questions.text: If multiple clients on the same OLT have bad light levels, where do you escalate the issue?
Question: What does this indicate and where should this be escalated?
questions.adminNotes:
• Multiple bad light levels = area issue.
  • Escalate to Config and Analyze.

questions.text: Explain why a speed test may not always reflect the customer's full package speed.
questions.adminNotes: • Because the test only shows what bandwidth is available at the time — other devices may be consuming bandwidth.

questions.text: If Preseem shows full throughput, but the customer says they have no devices connected, what could be the reason?
options: A hidden device may be using bandwidth
Fibre is too fast for Preseem to measure

questions.text: What is the last step in the troubleshooting flow if the issue is unresolved after all checks?
options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

questions.text:

  • The customer confirms there children are on school vacation

questions.text:

  • The customers power for the equipment is stable

questions.text: What is the purpose of Preseem ?

answers.0: No, the agent did not follow the correct procedure. Should the customer not answer after the first initial call back the agent needs to send the first email stating that a call attempt was made, the number dailed, the time and date of the call, request an alternative number or a better time to call. the template is available under email templates. Subsequent call attempts must be made after every two hours within an 8 hour shift. in total 4 calls attempts to be made within a shift. after each unsuccessful attempt an email following the same structure as the initial email to be sent. after the 4 failed attempt within a shift and still no response send a final email notyfying the client multiple call attempts were made, the ticket will be closed now due to no response. client may reach out again if assistance is still needed. all templates available on the email templates

answers.2: Acknowledge their frustration to show care for their issue and ask questions to show active engagement with the customer.
answers.5: Firstly advise the customer to switch to a LAN cable if they can, and if to make sure that only the devices that will be running the speed test is the only device connected at the time of testing.
answers.10: It indicates that during the speed test the customer was able to reach the peak usage of their package indicating that they have full use of their bandwidth package. Therefore yes it aligns with a typical speed test run.
answers.11: That is because speeds tests typically run a test on the download link first then run an upload link thereafter.
answers.13: The parallel connection to min 2 and the rest duration for the min and max to 180.
answers.15: Yes the test result is complete and valid. This is indicated by the font shifting from a grey tint into a solid black font color.
answers.17: So that it can restart the networks with the proper channels available to it and to ensure that that settings are properly committed and initialized.
answers.19: Auto channel for the 2.4GHz network should be set to true
answers.23: After confirming that the customer's light is high it would be best the observe other clients light levels on the same EPON, if there are more clients with light levels above -27 then escalate the matter to CONFIG & ANALYSIS but if it is only one customer than escalate it to field operations.
answers.25: It indicates that there is an issue the EPON of that area which is affecting more than one customer therefore it has to be sent to CONFIG & ANALYSIS.
answers.26: Because in the event that another device is using the bandwidth while the customer is running a speed test it would affect the results displayed at the end of the speed test. But more importantly a speed tests numbers might be slight lower than the package because of transfer rates and how speed is calculated.
answers.29: It is to keep the customer engaged as well to educate the customer on what can be done the next time the issue arises. This helps reduce calls of the same issue as more clients will know what to do in the event of a slow speed issue.

• Use phrases like “I understand how frustrating that must be.”
  • Actively listen and avoid interrupting.

questions.text: Why is it important to ask a client to test using a LAN cable?
options: It helps determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-related or affects all mediums.
LAN tests are required for documentation

questions.text: If a customer does not have band steering enabled, what should they test?
What physical setup should you confirm with the customer to rule out interference?
questions.adminNotes:
• Confirm the client has clear line of sight to the router.
  • Ensure the router is not placed on top of electronics.

questions.text: Which monitoring tool is used to view real-time throughput?
Why should the metrics view be set to “Last 15 minutes”?
options: To see average usage for the month over a 15 minute interval
To compare it to previous days usage

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • A speed test shows available bandwidth, not total capacity.

questions.text: If a speed test shows 50 Mbps but Preseem shows 100 Mbps during the same period, what does it indicate?
options: Another device on the network is using bandwidth

questions.text: Question: What do you observe in this graph? Does it align with a typical speed test run?
questions.adminNotes:
• Identify if throughput spikes during speed test period.
  • Confirm if it matches expected package speeds.

questions.text: Explain why the upload appears later than the download in the Preseem metrics graph while doing a speed test.
questions.adminNotes: • The test runs download first, then upload — so Preseem will reflect upload slightly later.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct speed test link?
What setting must be changed in the speed test to run a 6-minute test?
questions.adminNotes:
• Set parallel connections to minimum 2
  • Set test duration to 180 seconds for both min and max

questions.text: What happens after the download portion of the 6-minute test finishes?
Question: Based on this result, is the test complete and valid? How can you tell?
questions.adminNotes:
• All speed test results are no longer greyed out.
  • Download and upload values are shown clearly.

questions.text: What setting should be enabled on the router for optimal channel selection?
Why is it beneficial to reboot the router after enabling Auto Channel?
questions.adminNotes: • It allows the router to pick the best channel based on current interference when it reboots.

questions.text: Which wireless standard operates on the 5 GHz band?
Question: What change should be made here to improve performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Auto Channel is set to “False.”
  • Should change to “True” and commit the change.

questions.text: After changing the Auto Channel setting to “True,” what must you do next?
What is the acceptable light level range for fibre connections?
Where do you check a customer’s fibre light levels?
What action must be taken if a customer’s light level is -29 dBm?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Field Operations immediately.

questions.text: If multiple clients on the same OLT have bad light levels, where do you escalate the issue?
Question: What does this indicate and where should this be escalated?
questions.adminNotes:
• Multiple bad light levels = area issue.
  • Escalate to Config and Analyze.

questions.text: Explain why a speed test may not always reflect the customer's full package speed.
questions.adminNotes: • Because the test only shows what bandwidth is available at the time — other devices may be consuming bandwidth.

questions.text: If Preseem shows full throughput, but the customer says they have no devices connected, what could be the reason?
options: A hidden device may be using bandwidth
Fibre is too fast for Preseem to measure

questions.text: What is the last step in the troubleshooting flow if the issue is unresolved after all checks?
options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

answers.1: Showing them you understand their problem and you are willing to help them get the problem resolved.
answers.2: Listen and understand the customers problem and acknowledge the problem.- Check via CHIP if the customer is affected by an outage. Confirm if the client has an active job card.
answers.3: Confirm first which site is affected by the outage. Let the client know they are part of an outage, and reassure them that technicians are working on getting them back online again. If an update is available on teams provide them with the latest update.
answers.4: Confirm if there is an electrical outage in the area to rule out why the CPE's are not switching on and confirming if it could be damaged equipment.
answers.5: the next step would be to escalate to Field operation, but before doing so you must also confirm if its just one client affected on that EPON. If its multiple it needs to be escalated to config and analyse.
answers.7: The cause could be the clients device. but to rule it out the client must try to connect to their network using different devices. Should other devices be able to access the network than surely the fault lies with that one device and it needs to be checked.
answers.8: Ping the AP and the ODU to see if you get a response. Should no response come back it needs to be escalated to Config and analyse
answers.11: It means that between the AP and the customers ODU the connection is lost or something in between is damaged.
answers.16: The router/ gateway was reset to its original default settings. It needs to be reconfigured.
answers.17: The clients device WiFi toggle is switched off. Meaning that the device will not have access to the internet till it gets switched on.

options: To see average usage for the month over a 15 minute interval

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • A speed test shows available bandwidth, not total capacity.

options: Another device on the network is using bandwidth

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct speed test link?

questions.text: Which wireless standard operates on the 5 GHz band?

questions.text:

questions.adminNotes:
• The customer is utilizing there full bandwidth that the package offers
  • Agent educates the customer on how bandwidth works

questions.text:

  • The customers power for the equipment is stable

questions.adminNotes:
• The customers ODU , in home & AP has high latency indicating a problm on the AP

questions.text:

  • What is the next recommended step ?
questions.adminNotes:

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in stating that when they perform a speed test via there computer connected with a ethernet cable then the speed is correct. But when they watch tv which is connected via wi-fi it buffers. Speed tests performed via the Wi-Fi is also significantly lower then the ethernet test. questions.adminNotes:
• Distance from device to router
  • Freqeuncy to which the device is connected

questions.text:

questions.adminNotes:

A password error in Outlook may mean:
options: The antivirus is out of date
The email account is blocked or the password is incorrect

answers.0: The possible cause here is that the fibre light is above -27 meaning the cables are not getting the fibre light from the Herotel network, therefore this is causing the slow speed from the customers side that is why they are not reaching their full bandwidth. Another reason would be that the cables are damaged. Next step is to further investigate the customers ODU and escalate this to fields operations if this is only affecting the one client, but if multiple then we will escalate to config & analyze.
answers.1: This metrics indicates a high usage of bandwidth, it could be that the customer is downloading huge files from the network that is above their package. I would educate the customer not to download huge files from their network and that they are over utilizing their package, they might consider to upgrade their package.
answers.2: This matrics indicates that the yellow line, green line and the blue line are spiking and it looks like the customer is maxing out their package and there is a bottleneck level, therefore the latency is high and this is also causing the intermittency and network congestion. This means the issue needs to be escalated to config & analyze.
answers.3: This metrics indicates a bad health on Health score and RF Score, meaning there is an issue between the AP and the customer that is connecting to that AP, this needs to be escalated to config & analyze.
answers.4: The customer needs to disconnect all devices that are connected via their wi-fi internet connection, try to test again using the LAN cable to get the best results of the internet. However if they are trying to test wirelessly, they need to connect to 5G and stand in front of the router to get their full speed with other devices being disconnected still.
answers.5: False, fibre speed test provides the results only after 2 minutes.
answers.6: I would explain to the customer that the reason for this might be that there are other devices sharing the same bandwidth as them that is why we're getting less bandwidth when we're trying to do the speed test.
answers.9: Wireless medium, the blue line indicates the Access Point the customer is connecting to.
answers.10: It indicates that there is an issue with the customers CPE and it needs to be investigated further, the access point is fine but the latency is high and the orange and green line are spiking and not stable. Possible congestion from the customers side.
answers.11: This is a wireless throughput graph that shows the download and upload rate of a customers package, and here it indicates that the customer is reaching their package limit for the upload rate which is 2Mbps but almost reaching 5 Mbps for download rate.
answers.12: This metrics shows a good health on Health Score and RF Score meaning there is most likely no issue with the access point, if the customer is still experiencing a slow speed, then the issue is not within the sectors side, it is most possibly from the customers side, faulty CPE or the device that is connected to the network itself.

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating they are expereincing slow speed. The following steps have been performed :

  • The speed test results does not match the customers package even via ethernet

questions.adminNotes:

questions.text:

questions.text:

What further steps can be performed with the customer to narrow down if the problem is via the Wi-Fi or if the network in general could be expereincing the problem ?
questions.adminNotes:

  • Freqeuncy to which the device is connected

questions.text:

questions.adminNotes:
• False
  • Fibre customers poll every 2 minutes as they have not gone inline

answers.2: Confirm the account status, confirm if the client is part of an active outage, check for any active job cards or an existing ticket.
answers.5: Confirm if its just one client/ multiple on the OLT/PON that is affected- If its one escalate to field operations, multiple escalate to Config and Analyze

answers.8: Ping the AP IP address to confirm if its reachable on the Herotel network or Preseem is unable to poll the AP
answers.11: Something between the AP and ODU could be damaged, or the connection could be lost
answers.14: ONT/ONU- Converts Fibre signal into copper (electrical) signal
answers.16: The gateway/router was reset to its original default settings
answers.17: The clients WiFi is disabled on the device.

answers.2:
1. We can confirm if a customer is affected by an outage on Chip where it shows the status of an active network outage
2. We can confirm by searching on teams outage & maintanance channels with the customers location or site on Chip
3. We can also check on Preseem if there is a wire down
4. Command Prompt to ping the AP IP address and see if it is responding
answers.3: If the customers ODU is not connected meaning the AP is offline, then this means the customer is not connected to the Herotel network, and there are no packets travelling between the customers network and herotels network. Therefore, I will have to check if there is a possible network outage on Chip, and if the customer has any active sessions on Radius Server, if not then I will check if this is affecting only one customer or multiple customers on Preseem, if only one customer is affected, I will escalate to fields operations, but if multiple customers are affected, then I will escalate to config & analyse.
answers.10:
1. The cables are plugged in incorrectly, use the install standards guide to confirm the cable connection
2. The POE does not have power
3. Possible network outage
4. The antenna reset or is damaged
answers.12: The status of the customers ADS states that the is no data available in the table. This means there is no current active session on this account, it could be that the account has been suspended or there is an active outage occurring in that area or the account has been cancelled.

rows: The Backhaul is offline
The NAS ran out of backup power during loadshedding
There was a break in by the tower and equipment was stolen
A upstream provider has a cable break.

questions.text: What is the recommended way to greet a customer on a support call?
options: Thank you for calling, this is [Name].
Thank you for calling Herotel Technical Support, [Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?
Hey, what seems to be the problem?

questions.text: What is the purpose of smiling while speaking on a call?
options: It makes your voice sound friendly and enthusiastic

questions.text: List two ways to show empathy and build rapport with the customer during a call.
questions.adminNotes:
• “I understand this must be frustrating.”
  • Use active listening and repeat their issue back.
  • Friendly tone, no sighs or frustration.

questions.text: Why do we ask the customer to test using a LAN cable?
options: Speed tests only works on LAN
To determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-specific or across all mediums

questions.text: If the customer does not have band steering enabled, which frequencies must they test?
What two physical factors around the router could affect wireless performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Router location: not behind objects, in cupboards, or stacked.
  • Avoid interference from TVs, microwaves, and fish tanks.

questions.text: Which tool is used to monitor real-time wireless throughput?
Why should we change the metrics view from 2 weeks to 15 minutes in Preseem?
options: To better match the speed test timeframe
It shows the average across all users

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • Available bandwidth at a specific moment — not the full capacity of the package.

questions.text: In Preseem, what does it mean if the yellow line meets the green line?
options: The client is experiencing packet loss
The client is maxing out their plan

questions.text: Question: What does this graph tell you about the customer’s usage?
questions.adminNotes: • Customer is using full package speed — no fault on the ISP side.

questions.text: What should you ask the customer to do before analysing throughput?
questions.adminNotes: • Ask the customer to run 3 consecutive speed tests and inform you of the times.

questions.text: What Wi-Fi setting allows the router to automatically choose the best frequency band?
Why should we ask the customer to reboot the router after enabling auto channel?
questions.adminNotes: • To apply the new wireless settings and ensure the router reselects optimal channels.

What does good RF and good health indicate?

What do bad RF and bad health score indicate?
options: the frequency needs to be changed to make it better
Problem between AP and client — escalate to config for further analysis.
the client needs to move the ODU to make it better

questions.text: Question: What does this tell you about the sector?
questions.adminNotes: • Sector experiencing performance issues — possibly oversubscribed or congested.

questions.text: If latency is only spiking on the client and not the sector, who do you escalate to?
options: Config and Analyze to change the clients frequency
Field Operations to check for interference or alignment problems.
Area manager to call the client and calm him down

questions.text: What should you do if both the customer and sector show unstable latency?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Config and Analyze to check channel width, power, and access point settings.

questions.text: If speed test from the sector to client is below package speed, what must be done?
When all tests are complete and no fault is found, what is your next step?
questions.adminNotes: • Educate the customer on Wi-Fi limitations, device usage, and recommend testing on LAN.

questions.text: What’s the main benefit of reviewing latency graphs on both customer and sector?
options: It tells you what equipment they have
It identifies where the fault lies

questions.text: Question: What should you conclude from this and what action is required?
questions.adminNotes: • Indicates poor signal and stability — escalate to config and possibly field ops.

answers.0:
1. The cause of the customer's problem is a high fibre light level (Above -27).
2. The next step would be to check if this high fibre light level is affecting multiple customers or only this specific customer. If it's affecting multiple customers, this issue needs to be escalated to config and analyse, if it's affecting only one customer the issue should be escalated to field operations.
answers.1: This metrics indicate that the customer often uses their full package allocations and sometimes even exceeds the allocated download and upload data. I would educate the customer by explaining to them how package allocations and package speeds work, and what it means to exceed the allocated data amount, aswell as the effect that it may have on the connection (example: slow speeds and poor network quality).
answers.2:
1. This metrics indicates that the AP is experiencing latency variation (unstable latency) and the latency keeps spiking. If the AP that a customer's ODU is experiencing latency variation the customers ODU and in home network connection will also experience the unstable latency which will cause the customer to experience slow speeds. 
2. Latency is a measure of data transfer seed (how quickly data packets get from a starting point to their destination).
answers.3: These metrics indicate that both the AP's Health and RF scores are poor. This needs to be escalated to config and analyse as a result.
answers.4:
1. The customer can connect the TV straight to the gateway via a LAN cable to see if it still buffers, if it runs smoothly, the issue is wi-fi specific.
2. The customer could test using another device and connecting that device to the gateway using a LAN cable and connect to it wirelessly to see if the speed is the same o if it differs.
3. We can ask the customer to perform a wireless signal strength test to see if the wi-fi signal is poor or strong.
answers.5: False, it takes up to 6 minutes for an accurate speed test to complete. A 10 second speed test will showcase inaccurate results and the stats cannot be used for troubleshooting.
answers.6: I will notify the customer that different types of interferences exist that could affect the outcome of a speed test whereas we see actual accurate usage data on our-side therefore a slight difference could occur.
answers.9: The medium that provides a latency graph is the wireless medium and the blue line indicates the latency of the AP that the customer is connected to.
answers.10: The graph indicates that the AP latency is stable and therefore the issue is not with the Herotel network itself. We can therefor comfortably say that the issue is with the customers in-home network and it could be a result of an outdated or damaged ODU.
answers.11: The following throughput graph shows that the customer uses less than their allocated package.
answers.12: Based on the attatched metrics, the problem could possibly be originating from the customer's in-home network. These stats show that the AP is doing ok and the issue cannot be with the Herotel network as a result. The issue could be due to line over-utilization or an outdated/damaged ODU.

Once a call has ended without a customer's issue being resolved, the agent should attempt to call the customer back every 2 hours within the agents 8 hour shift. The email should include the number dialed, the time and date of the call and a request for the customer to provide a better time to call or an alternative number to call on.

answers.19:

* Queue and commit the change

this is not a Problem however if the test is drastically off this indicates a Problem.

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • A speed test shows available bandwidth, not total capacity.

questions.text: If a speed test shows 50 Mbps but Preseem shows 100 Mbps during the same period, what does it indicate?
options: Another device on the network is using bandwidth

questions.text: Question: What do you observe in this graph? Does it align with a typical speed test run?
questions.adminNotes:
• Identify if throughput spikes during speed test period.
  • Confirm if it matches expected package speeds.

questions.text: Explain why the upload appears later than the download in the Preseem metrics graph while doing a speed test.
questions.adminNotes: • The test runs download first, then upload — so Preseem will reflect upload slightly later.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct speed test link?
What setting must be changed in the speed test to run a 6-minute test?
questions.adminNotes:
• Set parallel connections to minimum 2
  • Set test duration to 180 seconds for both min and max

questions.text: What happens after the download portion of the 6-minute test finishes?
Question: Based on this result, is the test complete and valid? How can you tell?
questions.adminNotes:
• All speed test results are no longer greyed out.
  • Download and upload values are shown clearly.

questions.text: What setting should be enabled on the router for optimal channel selection?
Why is it beneficial to reboot the router after enabling Auto Channel?
questions.adminNotes: • It allows the router to pick the best channel based on current interference when it reboots.

questions.text: Which wireless standard operates on the 5 GHz band?
Question: What change should be made here to improve performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Auto Channel is set to “False.”
  • Should change to “True” and commit the change.

questions.text: After changing the Auto Channel setting to “True,” what must you do next?
What is the acceptable light level range for fibre connections?
Where do you check a customer’s fibre light levels?
What action must be taken if a customer’s light level is -29 dBm?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Field Operations immediately.

questions.text: If multiple clients on the same OLT have bad light levels, where do you escalate the issue?
Question: What does this indicate and where should this be escalated?
questions.adminNotes:
• Multiple bad light levels = area issue.
  • Escalate to Config and Analyze.

questions.text: Explain why a speed test may not always reflect the customer's full package speed.
questions.adminNotes: • Because the test only shows what bandwidth is available at the time — other devices may be consuming bandwidth.

questions.text: If Preseem shows full throughput, but the customer says they have no devices connected, what could be the reason?
options: A hidden device may be using bandwidth
Fibre is too fast for Preseem to measure

questions.text: What is the last step in the troubleshooting flow if the issue is unresolved after all checks?
options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

questions.text: What is the differance between TCP and UDP ?
options: TCP is connection orreinted , when a packet can be sent or was received a acknowledgement takes place
Both TCP & UDP is ideal for VoIP traffic
UDP send & receives packets without acknowledgements
TCP adjusts the packets size and adjust the bandwidth speed at which packets are sent
UDP is ideal for sending a email
TCP provides the gateway with a external IP address
UDP provides the gateway with a external IP address
TCP provides error checking. Packets received are put into the correct order. In the case of a missing packet it will reqeust a re-send of that packet.

rows: New customers get processed through this department , the account is created and send to Field Operations for instillation.

Example – A sector is offline due to cable or equipment damage and needs to be replaced.
Example – A outage affecting multiple fibre customers connected on the same Run is offline because a truck drove through the cable.

Scenario: You have received the following communication from a client Good day to whom it may concern Name: Juan Prince. Account code: IGJAC145 My connection has been unstable for the last 2 months every night after 8pm When i watch DSTV live channels it keeps on buffering while watching rugby game, i have tired contacting your support team but nobody answers, i already rebooted the router a few times now with confirming the small black box even reseated the cables my Lan is running from the black box on the gigabyte data to the Yellow WAN of the Router and the one in Gigabyte Data power goes outside, it would appears to be connecting to the Dish outside, i have a black Router with Herotel written in white on top of the router when my rugby starts buffering its only on my tv on which i check my phone and other devices is working perfectly fine, i only have this one router on my premises when i run a speed test from my laptop which is cabled directly to my router i am getting 4 Mbps download and 2 Mbps upload from ookla speed test The smart tv is connected via Cable as well, i have also received a WhatsApp that there is Maintenance planned for next week, the only way to contact me is via email juanprincehero@consistency.key 
  • Create a ticket regarding your customers description to the helpdesk standard ticket creation 
  • Template : 
Name: 
Contact number: 
Site Location: 
Description of the customer's problem: 
Start time / Time frame: 
Rebooted Equipment (Y/N) : 
Router Type : 
Wireless or LAN devices affected : 
SSID visible (Y/N/D) : 
Devices affected :
Multiple Routers (Y/N) : 
If multiple routers – problem on all routers or certain routers : 
Confirm Power to ONU / CPE (Y/N) : 
Wire down (Y/N) : Website if access problem : 
Cables confirmed (Y/N) : 
Active outage confirmation step for the area completed (Y/N) :

1. added ticket template the the relevant field 
1. Fully filled in the template as per the scenario

questions.text:
Explain the following Transfer methods and there function 
- Blind Transfer
- Warm Transfer
questions.modelAnswer:
1. Blind Transfer: The call will imediatly transfer to that user or department
1. Warm Transfer: The customer will be placed on hold , then the user or department the transfer must take place to will be pulled into the communication (customer still on hold) , the reason for transfer will be explained then the communication can be transferred

answers.0: The client fibre light is -36.5, confirm if its just one/multiple client affected on the same PON/OLT. If its one escalate to Field operation- multiple escalate to Config and Analyze
answers.1: Client is utilizing their full bandwidth- advice client on how bandwidth works/ connect less devices at a time and disconnect the once that are not in use at the moment.
answers.2: There is a high latency on the ODU, AP and in home. The problem lies on the AP. Latency is the amount of time packets take to travel from point a to b.
answers.3: Both health and RF score is bad which means the problem lies on the AP- escalate to config and analyze.
answers.4: They need to check on which frequency they are connected to (2.4G OR 5G). Verify the distance from the router to the devices that are connected wirelessly.
answers.5: False, fibre polls metrics every two minutes.
answers.6: Preseem shows everything being used on the network, including all devices and background activity, speed tests shows only the available bandwidth at the moment
answers.9: Wireless- the blue line is the AP.
answers.10: The problem lies between the ODU and in home- The ODU could be unaligned or damaged
answers.11: The client does not use their full bandwidth or possible the ODU does not receive the full bandwidth.
answers.12: The problem is most likely on the customers side it could be device limitations or WiFi interference.

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that there connection feels slow and at times very intermitted. You have performed the following troubleshooting steps :
  • The customer is not part of a outage affecting the AP they are connecting to
  • The customers RF score is 4.07 & ok
  • The customers power for the equipment is stable 
  • The preseem metrics are as attached
1. What do the metrics indicate ?
2. What is latency ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The customers ODU , in home & AP has high latency indicating a problm on the AP
  • Latency is how long packets take from point a to reach point b

questions.text:
During the troubleshooting process you open the AP metrics (find the metrics attached).
  • What do the metrics indicate ?
  • What is the next recommended step ?
questions.adminNotes:
• There is a problem on the Ap as both the health & RF score is bad.
  • Escalate to C & A

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in stating that when they perform a speed test via there computer connected with a ethernet cable then the speed is correct. But when they watch tv which is connected via wi-fi it buffers. Speed tests performed via the Wi-Fi is also significantly lower then the ethernet test. Keep in mind the customer has a 100 Mbps symetrical fibre connection.
What further steps can be performed with the customer to narrow down if the problem is via the Wi-Fi or if the network in general could be expereincing the problem ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Distance from device to router
  • Freqeuncy to which the device is connected

questions.text:
True / False : A fibre customer can perform a speed test and 10 seconds later the speed test results will populate in the metrics.

questions.adminNotes:
• False

questions.text:

questions.text: What is the differance between TCP and UDP ?
options: TCP is connection orreinted , when a packet can be sent or was received a acknowledgement takes place
Both TCP & UDP is ideal for VoIP traffic
UDP send & receives packets without acknowledgements
TCP adjusts the packets size and adjust the bandwidth speed at which packets are sent
UDP is ideal for sending a email
TCP provides the gateway with a external IP address
UDP provides the gateway with a external IP address
TCP provides error checking. Packets received are put into the correct order. In the case of a missing packet it will reqeust a re-send of that packet.

questions.modelAnswer: False , A Gateway is being described not a AP / Bridge

rows: New customers get processed through this department , the account is created and send to Field Operations for instillation.
Problems that affect customers internal networks that originate from the Herotel network / Upstream providers / break out point(s) gets escalated to this department.
Credit requests with a report of what caused the problem and why credit is warranted can be sent here.
This department is responsible for scheduling technicians to assist with a problem the customer is experiencing on the premises if Remote Support could not resolve the problem.
This department is responsible for performing remote troubleshooting steps to resolve a problem remotely without additional costs (situation dependant).
Example – A sector is offline due to cable or equipment damage and needs to be replaced.
Example – A outage affecting multiple fibre customers connected on the same Run is offline because a truck drove through the cable.

answers.6: The Dome was on the pole and had multiple drop cables giving clients access to the Herotel network. Removing the Dome disconnects clients or even cutting the drop cables will disconnect the clients.
answers.7: EMI- the electrical currents are what is causing it.
answers.9: Half duplex- transmission can not be sent and received at the same time.
answers.10: The gateway. Devices in bridge mode can not perform NAT functions.
answers.17: MAC addrees/ MAC Serial number
answers.22: 3 failed authentication attempts will result in a 5 minute bucket blacklist.
answers.24: The client is authenticating on a different realm- escalate to Config and Analyze
answers.26: account code
answers.29: Domain is suspended
answers.31: the email deliverability check is invalid instead of being valid- escalate to Config and Analyze
answers.32: Confirm if the domain still has available storage/ if another email address can be added
answers.34: No it was incorrect- 4 calls per 8 hour shift to be made. every two hours agent must try to call the client followed by an email stating the agent tried to make contact, with the time and date. followed by a ticket closure email after 4 call attempts.
answers.36: Change the department, ticket type, agent name, assign correctly
answers.37: Assist the client first- if agents fail escalate to TL who will try to assist and escalating to customer care if issue persist.

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they are expereincing slow speed , you have confirmed the following :
  • The customer confirms that when the wireless service was installed a few years ago there were no trees in front of the direction where the antenna is pointing however in the last few months he has noticed that the trees do now seem to be at the same height as his ODU. 
  • It has also been confirmed that the speed is slow in general its not just specific times
Identify the possible cause of the problem
questions.adminNotes: • LOS is possibly obstructed between the ODU & AP / PTMP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they are in the process of downloading a file from one drive. How ever the file is 1 Gb big and only downloading at 5 Mbps. The customer has a 10 Mbps download by 5 Mbps upload package. You log into the customers antenna and confirm that the RX traffic shows 10 Mbps. What does this indicate.
questions.adminNotes: • The full download bandwidth is being utilized

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they would like to extend there network connection to a work shop on the same premises. The following has been confirmed :
  • The work shop is 300 m from the original point of instalstion 
  • You have confirmed that a ethernet cable starts degrading packets at 70 m +-
What solution can be offered to the customer to resolve this problem
questions.adminNotes: • Suggest a PTP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer brings a damaged router into the office stating it has been hit by lighting. The customer does not wish to accumalate any aditional costs and supplies a wireless extender with 2 ariels into the office to be reconfigured as the Gateway. Why will this not resolve the problem.
options: The extender cannot support the customers bandwidth
The extender cannot be configured in Gateway mode
The extender was not supplied by Herotel
The extenders make & model is not well known

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer would like to extend his Wi-Fi connectivity to his lapa by his swimming pool. The following has been confirmed :
  • The lapa is 50 m from the original instilation point
  • The connecting the lapa's equipment to the house's equipment will be exposed to weather
What is the best cabling solution for the customers request
questions.adminNotes: • STP

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following : 
  • The customer has a fiber package
  • The ONT / ONU seems to not have power as the customer states there are no lights burning on it
  • The Gateway how ever does have lights burning and there is a ethernet connection present between the ONT / ONU & the Gateway
  • There is no active data session for the radius user
What is the cause of the customer not having a active data session and why is this your answer.
questions.adminNotes:
• ONT / ONU does not have power
  • There is nothing to convert the fibre signal received from the herotel network into a copper signal so that the gateway can comunicate to and from the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in and states that recently they have been doing some construction of a building in the back of their yard. They removed a pole which connected multiple fiber customers to the Herotel network.
  • Why did removing the pole disconnect multiple customers ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The pole has a dome which connects the drop cables into the customers premises
  • The dome also connects the customers to the rest of the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacted in and confirms that there internet feels slow. When they google or try to upload something to one drive it fails and multiple attempts needs to be made before it uploads. You have confirmed the following 
  • The customer is using UTP cabling on the premises which is a office
  • All power sources for the offices originate from one point to keep the cabling neat
  • The UTP cable that connects the access point to the switch runs over the power cabling
What is the possible cause of the problems being expereinced by the customer and why do you suspect this is the cause of the problem.
questions.adminNotes:
• EMI
  • EMI is caused by high currents

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer who has 5 flats in back of his yard contacts in stating that his renters have reported slow speed & intermitted connectivity when they are connected to the Wi-Fi. You have confirmed the following :
  • Each flat has there own access point with its own SSID credentials
  • Each access point is connected via a ethernet cable to a switch in the customers home
  • The flats are 10 m apart & only one story
What could be the possible cause of the slow speed / intermitted connectivty.
questions.adminNotes: • Wireless interference

questions.text:
Scenario : A customers contacts into the First Line Support department regarding slow speed & intermitted connectivty. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customers ethernet cable between the ODU & PoE is syncing at 10 Mbps

Why is this transmission method not optimal ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Half-Duplex
  • The device cannot sent & receive at the time which causes the network to feel slow

questions.text:

answers.2: Listen and understand their problem, Acknowledge their frustration

answers.10: The graph shows a high spike in download traffic followed by a spike in upload traffic. This aligns with a typical speed test because the download test runs first and once it finishes, the upload test starts, which is why the upload spike appears after the download.
answers.11: The upload appears later because the speed test first runs the download test and only starts the upload test after the download test is completed, so the upload traffic shows later on the graph.
answers.13: Test duration
answers.15: Yes the test is complete because upload, download and latency results are displayed
answers.17: Rebooting the router allows it to scan the environment and select the best available wireless channel with the least interference
answers.19: Auto channel should be changed from false to true, queued and committed
answers.23: Confirm if its just one client on OLT that is affected or multiple.
answers.25: It indicates that multiple clients on the same OLT have poor fibre light levels. This suggests a issue and should be escalated to Config and Analyse.
answers.26: A speed test may not show the full package speed because Wi-Fi interference, device limitations, background downloads, or other devices using bandwidth can affect the results.
answers.29: It helps the customer understand the troubleshooting process. It ensures the tests are done correctly on their side. It helps them identify and resolve similar issues in the future.

questions.text: Explain why a speed test may not always reflect the customer's full package speed.

options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

answers.0: Possible cause would be the trees acting as a physical barrier. They disrupt the signal coming from the High point(Tower)
answers.1: The customer is utilizing their entire line.
answers.2: Extenders must be installed on the premises. If not a separate gateway must be installed at the workshop.
answers.5: The ONT/ONU is off/ damaged, possible reason could be its not connected to a power source or the power source cable or the ONT/ONU could be damaged.
answers.6: The physical connection was damaged by cutting the pole. The pole could have been an Epon/ Dome. Cutting these line or the pole it self will results in multiple clients losing their connection.
answers.7: Interference could be causing the slow speed. The interference could be from Noise floor or even Wireless interference.
answers.8: Noise floor due to the amount of signals / Wireless ineterference- equipment that sends out microwaves such as TV's or microwaves
answers.9: Because it sends transmission in one direction and waits for the the session to complete before sending it back. It can not send and receive transmission at the same time
answers.10: On the main gateway. Devices configured in bridge mode can not act as a gateway nor perform gateway functions such as NAS translation.
answers.17: MAC address
answers.22: The device tried to authenticate previously and got rejected this could be due to incorrect SSID password and the device will be put on a waiting bucket
answers.24: A user being proxied means that they are not authenticating on the Herotel server but on Horetel business server. This needs to be escalated to Config and analyse
answers.25: The account code can be used if the client has multiple radiuses and the PPPOE can be used to confirm one certain radius
answers.26: Account code
answers.29: The account is suspended
answers.30: When a quota and disk is the same amount it means that there is no space/storage for more emails to be sent and received. The only way to resolve this would be to educate the client on deleting old emails or escalating the issue to sales and the client can receive a new quote for more storage.
answers.31: The deliverability was changed to "INVALID". This setting should always be set to valid
answers.32: By confirming if the clients domain is on CP 1 OR 2
answers.34: No, correct procedure would be to sent the 1st call attempt email stating the date and time the agent tried to call. This procedure is to be repeated 4 times in an 8 hour shift before the final email stating that the ticket is to be closed can be sent.
answers.36: change the department to national_sales. Change the agent name to a department. (national_sals)

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they are expereincing slow speed , you have confirmed the following :
  • The customer confirms that when the wireless service was installed a few years ago there were no trees in front of the direction where the antenna is pointing however in the last few months he has noticed that the trees do now seem to be at the same height as his ODU. 
  • It has also been confirmed that the speed is slow in general its not just specific times
Identify the possible cause of the problem
questions.adminNotes: • LOS is possibly obstructed between the ODU & AP / PTMP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they are in the process of downloading a file from one drive. How ever the file is 1 Gb big and only downloading at 5 Mbps. The customer has a 10 Mbps download by 5 Mbps upload package. You log into the customers antenna and confirm that the RX traffic shows 10 Mbps. What does this indicate.
questions.adminNotes: • The full download bandwidth is being utilized

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they would like to extend there network connection to a work shop on the same premises. The following has been confirmed :
  • The work shop is 300 m from the original point of instalstion 
  • You have confirmed that a ethernet cable starts degrading packets at 70 m +-
What solution can be offered to the customer to resolve this problem
questions.adminNotes: • Suggest a PTP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer brings a damaged router into the office stating it has been hit by lighting. The customer does not wish to accumalate any aditional costs and supplies a wireless extender with 2 ariels into the office to be reconfigured as the Gateway. Why will this not resolve the problem.
options: The extender cannot support the customers bandwidth
The extender cannot be configured in Gateway mode
The extender was not supplied by Herotel
The extenders make & model is not well known

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer would like to extend his Wi-Fi connectivity to his lapa by his swimming pool. The following has been confirmed :
  • The lapa is 50 m from the original instilation point
  • The connecting the lapa's equipment to the house's equipment will be exposed to weather
What is the best cabling solution for the customers request
questions.adminNotes: • STP

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following : 
  • The customer has a fiber package
  • The ONT / ONU seems to not have power as the customer states there are no lights burning on it
  • The Gateway how ever does have lights burning and there is a ethernet connection present between the ONT / ONU & the Gateway
  • There is no active data session for the radius user
What is the cause of the customer not having a active data session and why is this your answer.
questions.adminNotes:
• ONT / ONU does not have power
  • There is nothing to convert the fibre signal received from the herotel network into a copper signal so that the gateway can comunicate to and from the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in and states that recently they have been doing some construction of a building in the back of their yard. They removed a pole which connected multiple fiber customers to the Herotel network.
  • Why did removing the pole disconnect multiple customers ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The pole has a dome which connects the drop cables into the customers premises
  • The dome also connects the customers to the rest of the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacted in and confirms that there internet feels slow. When they google or try to upload something to one drive it fails and multiple attempts needs to be made before it uploads. You have confirmed the following 
  • The customer is using UTP cabling on the premises which is a office
  • All power sources for the offices originate from one point to keep the cabling neat
  • The UTP cable that connects the access point to the switch runs over the power cabling
What is the possible cause of the problems being expereinced by the customer and why do you suspect this is the cause of the problem.
questions.adminNotes:
• EMI
  • EMI is caused by high currents

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer who has 5 flats in back of his yard contacts in stating that his renters have reported slow speed & intermitted connectivity when they are connected to the Wi-Fi. You have confirmed the following :
  • Each flat has there own access point with its own SSID credentials
  • Each access point is connected via a ethernet cable to a switch in the customers home
  • The flats are 10 m apart & only one story
What could be the possible cause of the slow speed / intermitted connectivty.
questions.adminNotes: • Wireless interference

questions.text:
Scenario : A customers contacts into the First Line Support department regarding slow speed & intermitted connectivty. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customers ethernet cable between the ODU & PoE is syncing at 10 Mbps
  • The transmision method is described by the attached image
Why is this transmission method not optimal ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Half-Duplex
  • The device cannot sent & receive at the time which causes the network to feel slow

questions.text:
Scenario : A cutomer contacts in and states that they would like to configure port forwarding for their camera's that they can view them remotely. You have confirmed the following :
  • A wireless install is present on the premises
  • The ODU is configured in bridge mode
Where should the port forwarding rules be configured and why ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Gateway
  • The ODU is in bridge mode meaning the packets from the ODU pass on to the gateway , the gateway performs the natting

  • The customers ONU status is wire down

Scenario 1 : The agent must assign the ticket to the shift handover column

Scenario 2 : The agent notify's his or her team leader with the ticket number
Scenario 3 : The agent nofiy's his or her team leader with the ticket number
Scenario 3 : The agent ends his or her shift with the tickets still being assigned to him or her and continues working on the ticket during their next shift
Scenario 3 : The agents assigns all remaining tickets equily between the next shifts team members

answers.0: The possible cause of this problem is, the line of site between the AP and the customer's ODU is being obstructed by the trees.
answers.1: This indicates that the customer might be running another download in the background without being aware, or there could be a whole other device on the network that's downloading something at the same time.
answers.4: The best cabling solution for this customer's request would be a shielded twisted pair cable (STP).
answers.6: The pole likely had a PON setup on it. The PON is responsible for receiving fibre light from the OLT and then splitting and sending fiber light to it's correct location. Without a connected PON to receive fibre light from the OLT and distribute it, the fibre customers will not receive any fibre light and will therefore not be able to communicate to the Herotel network.
answers.7: The customer could be experiencing electromagnetic interference. The customer is using a UTP cable so this indicates that there is little to no insulation protecting the customer's twisted pair cable from the electromagnetic radiation that could be generated by the power cables that the UTP cable is running over. This interference will cause degraded signal quality which would explain why the customers internet feels slow.
answers.8: The flats are too close in proximity causing an overlap in signal.
answers.9: The transmission method described above is half-duplex. In a half-duplex data can either only be sent or received by a device at a time, it cannot send and receive simultaneously. This transmission method is not optimal because the user's device is unable to send and receive data simultaneously which makes the connection feel slow or intermittent when doing tasks that require simultaneous data transmission.
answers.10: The port forwarding rules should be configured on the gateway using ACS. This is because the gateway is responsible for sending data packets to the correct device connected to it on the network.
answers.17: I would locate the customers router on ACS using the WAN IP method.
answers.22: This error indicates that the client was unable to successfully authenticate to the radius server, and this is usually caused by using incorrect or non-existent login details more than once.
answers.24: The cause of this log entry is- A Herotel business customer is trying to authenticate the normal Herotel radius server. Herotel business has it's own sever and the authentication attempt needs to be proxied to that server in-order to solve the issue.
answers.25: The details that can be used to confirm an active session are, the customer's account code and their PPPoE username. You use the account could if the customer has multiple installations that you'd like to check and you use the PPPoE username if you're only checking the active data session for a single installation.
answers.26: I would use the Account code.
answers.29: The customer's domain is suspended, this is apparent by the red coloured panel.
answers.30: The customer has no storage left over as they used the allocated storage up meaning they've reached their limit.
answers.31: The problem could be caused by incorrectly configured SMTP settings, the issue can be resolved by using the correct SMTP settings and ensuring that they're configured correctly.
answers.32: We need to confirm wether the customer has owns the domain. If the customer owns the domain, we can assist in resolving the issue.

The two types of twisted pair cables are:
.Shielded twisted pair cable and;
.Unshielded twisted pair cable
Shielded twisted pair cables are commonly used in outdoor environments due to that fact that the cables are more protected and insulated. On the other hand, unshielded twisted pair cables offer a lot less shielding and insulation and is therefore commonly used in indoor environments.

answers.1: These metrics indicate that the customer's full download and upload bandwidth is being used and sometimes even exceeded. I would educate the customer by explaining to them what bandwidth and package speeds are and what happens when your usage matches or exceeds your package speeds.
answers.2:
1.)The metrics indicate that the latency of the AP that a customer is connected to is fluctuating (in other words the latency is high) which would explain why the customer's in home latency is bad as well and why they're experiencing slow/intermittent bandwidth.
2.)Latency is a measure of how long it takes for packets to get from one point to another.
answers.3: This metrics indicate a bad AP health and RF score. Everyone connected to this AP will experience high latency or even complete network drops, the next recommended step is to escalate the issue to config and analyse.
answers.4: The customer could move the device or the router closer to each other because distance between the device and the router can play a big part in slow speed due to signal getting weaker over distance. The customer could also test by running another speed test on the laptop but this time with the laptop connected wirelessly and not via cable, if the results differ significantly from the LAN connection vs the wireless connection, this indicates that the problem might be a Wi-Fi issue.
answers.5: False. Fibre is not inline therefore it takes 2 minutes for accurate results to populate. it's therefore recommended to run the speed test for at least 2 minutes (180 seconds).
answers.6: I would start of by explaining transfer rates to the customer and informing them about the minor discrepencies that could occur as a result of transfer rates and other calculating factors like interference. I would also explain to the customer how speed test results can differ from device to device based on that devices capabilities.

answers.10: This indicates that the customer's in home latency and their ODU latency is bad/spiking and this could indicate a damaged or out of spec ODU.
answers.11: The following throughput graph indicates that the customer is not receiving their full package bandwidth and their throughput is quite a lot lower than it should be. This could be an indication that the customers odu is damaged or out of spec.
answers.12: The problam is originating from the customers inhome and ODU. The AP health and RF scores are good so it's not an AP problem.

options: Thank you for calling, this is [Name].
Thank you for calling Herotel Technical Support, [Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?

options: It makes your voice sound friendly and enthusiastic

• “I understand this must be frustrating.”
  • Use active listening and repeat their issue back.
  • Friendly tone, no sighs or frustration.

questions.text: Why do we ask the customer to test using a LAN cable?

To determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-specific or across all mediums

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • Available bandwidth at a specific moment — not the full capacity of the package.

questions.text: In Preseem, what does it mean if the yellow line meets the green line?
options: The client is experiencing packet loss
The client is maxing out their plan

questions.text: Question: What does this graph tell you about the customer’s usage?

questions.text: What should you ask the customer to do before analysing throughput?

questions.text: What Wi-Fi setting allows the router to automatically choose the best frequency band?

the client needs to move the ODU to make it better

questions.text: Question: What does this tell you about the sector?
questions.adminNotes: • Sector experiencing performance issues — possibly oversubscribed or congested.

questions.text: If latency is only spiking on the client and not the sector, who do you escalate to?
options: Config and Analyze to change the clients frequency
Field Operations to check for interference or alignment problems.
Area manager to call the client and calm him down

questions.text: What should you do if both the customer and sector show unstable latency?

questions.adminNotes: • Educate the customer on Wi-Fi limitations, device usage, and recommend testing on LAN.

answers.8:
- Switch off the desk phone for 10-15 seconds by the wall socket where it is plugged into.
- Check as well that the power cable of the desk phone is properly seated in the phone itself as well as the wall socket. 
- Check that all the lights are completely off and then switch it back on.
answers.9: It is important because the VOIP protocol works via IPv4 and if the VOIP device does not have an IPv4 address it will not communicate on the network
answers.10: You must check that the cordless phones cradles/charging stations have power

Common causes would be:
- The ethernet cable is not properly connected to the Internet/LAN port on the phone / base station or to the gateway / switch / access point.
- No DHCP lease or misconfigured network settings

answers.2: Ask the customer questions to gain a clear and concise understanding of their issue and reword what was said to you regarding their problem to confirm/show understanding. Also,  listen attentively and don't interrupt the client while they speaking. This makes the customer feel heard/understood which in turn builds trust and rapport.
answers.5: Multiple devices connected to the network/router and if the device used for the speed test is connected via LAN cable. All appliances that are near the router and LAN cable that also generate radio frequencies (e.g. Microwave) could also cause interference.
answers.10: It tells me that the customer is able to utilize the full bandwidth of their plan/package.
answers.11: You should ask the client if they are testing via Wi-Fi or Lan and if they answer "Wi-Fi" you should ask them to test via LAN. Then after, ask them to perform the speed test.
answers.13: It forces the router to immediately scan for and select the best/least-congested Wi-Fi channel.
answers.15: Both channels'(2.4Ghz & 5Ghz) "Auto channel Enabled" setting should be changed to "true" to enable the router to automatically select the best/least congested channel.
answers.20: It tells me that the AP/sector's latency is fluctuating under 100ms and somewhat unstable. Indicating that some "lag"/latency will be experienced by the whole sector. Noticing/"feeling" the latency would depend on what each customer is using the internet for.
answers.22: Escalate the ticket to Config and Analyze
answers.24: Confirm full service from the customer and close the ticket
answers.26: You should conclude that there is a problem between the AP and customers connected to the AP and an escalation to Config and Analyze is required.

answers.2: One can/should ACKNOWLEDGE EMOTIONS while offering a calming ear, ask thoughtful questions to gain deeper understanding, be patient(allow others/customer to speak without interrupting) and confirm understanding by rewording what the customer has said.
answers.5: Confirm that all other devices of the customer is disconnected other than the device used to conduct the test. Confirm that the device the customer is doing the test on/with is connected via LAN.
answers.10: What I observe is that the customer performed a speed test(download) roughly around 13:12 to 13:15 and upload test at roughly 13:15 to 13:18. Furthermore, I observed the customer can/has achieved a download and upload bandwidth of 100mbps. Yes, this aligns with a typical speed test run.
answers.11: This is because the download bandwidth test is performed first and then the upload bandwidth second, right after.
answers.13: The Parallel connection minimum setting must be changed to 2 and the test duration setting, for both minimum and maximum, must be set to 180 seconds.
answers.15: Yes, the test is complete and valid as none of the results shown are greyed-out and all results are in solid-Black.
answers.17: It is beneficial because it forces the router to re-scan the environment and select the least congested channel.
answers.19: The Auto channel for the 2.4Ghz should be set to "true" because if the router is configured incorrectly the customer will experience slow speed internally/ the router won't select the "best/optimal" channel automatically.
answers.23: If only the ONE customer's light level is -29(anything higher than -27) dBm, the ticket/problem must be escalated to field operations. If multiple customers also have the same reading, anything above -27 dBm, the agent should escalate it to Config and Analyze.
answers.25: This indicates that multiple customers have bad light readings and should be escalated to Config and Analyze.
answers.26: Due to the transfer rate and other calculating factors, speed test can show a couple of Megabytes less than what the package shows.
answers.29: It is important because, firstly, by guiding customer's through each test and configuration you will also be educating them for future references and reducing the time it takes to resolve the issue the next time around. Secondly, the customer in most cases won't have the correct/proper knowledge of troubleshooting and testing their network connection(s) from their side and thus perform steps/configurations incorrectly without the agent assisting them.

questions.text: What is the recommended way to greet a customer when answering a call?
options: Hello, Herotel technical, how can i assist?
Good Morning, Herotel, how can i help?
Thank you for calling, Herotel Technical Support, [Agent Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?
Herotel, [Agent Name] speaking. to whom am i speaking?

questions.text: Why is it important to smile while speaking to a customer over the phone?
options: It makes your voice sound more friendly and enthusiastic.

questions.text: List two ways you can show empathy to a customer during a call.
questions.adminNotes:
• Use phrases like “I understand how frustrating that must be.”
  • Actively listen and avoid interrupting.

questions.text: Why is it important to ask a client to test using a LAN cable?
options: It helps determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-related or affects all mediums.
LAN tests are required for documentation

questions.text: If a customer does not have band steering enabled, what should they test?
What physical setup should you confirm with the customer to rule out interference?
questions.adminNotes:
• Confirm the client has clear line of sight to the router.
  • Ensure the router is not placed on top of electronics.

questions.text: Which monitoring tool is used to view real-time throughput?
Why should the metrics view be set to “Last 15 minutes”?
options: To see average usage for the month over a 15 minute interval
To compare it to previous days usage

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • A speed test shows available bandwidth, not total capacity.

questions.text: If a speed test shows 50 Mbps but Preseem shows 100 Mbps during the same period, what does it indicate?
options: Another device on the network is using bandwidth

questions.text: Question: What do you observe in this graph? Does it align with a typical speed test run?
questions.adminNotes:
• Identify if throughput spikes during speed test period.
  • Confirm if it matches expected package speeds.

questions.text: Explain why the upload appears later than the download in the Preseem metrics graph while doing a speed test.
questions.adminNotes: • The test runs download first, then upload — so Preseem will reflect upload slightly later.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct speed test link?
What setting must be changed in the speed test to run a 6-minute test?
questions.adminNotes:
• Set parallel connections to minimum 2
  • Set test duration to 180 seconds for both min and max

questions.text: What happens after the download portion of the 6-minute test finishes?
Question: Based on this result, is the test complete and valid? How can you tell?
questions.adminNotes:
• All speed test results are no longer greyed out.
  • Download and upload values are shown clearly.

questions.text: What setting should be enabled on the router for optimal channel selection?
Why is it beneficial to reboot the router after enabling Auto Channel?
questions.adminNotes: • It allows the router to pick the best channel based on current interference when it reboots.

questions.text: Which wireless standard operates on the 5 GHz band?
Question: What change should be made here to improve performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Auto Channel is set to “False.”
  • Should change to “True” and commit the change.

questions.text: After changing the Auto Channel setting to “True,” what must you do next?

Where do you check a customer’s fibre light levels?

questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Field Operations immediately.

questions.text: If multiple clients on the same OLT have bad light levels, where do you escalate the issue?

questions.adminNotes:
• Multiple bad light levels = area issue.
  • Escalate to Config and Analyze.

questions.text: Explain why a speed test may not always reflect the customer's full package speed.
questions.adminNotes: • Because the test only shows what bandwidth is available at the time — other devices may be consuming bandwidth.

questions.text: If Preseem shows full throughput, but the customer says they have no devices connected, what could be the reason?
options: A hidden device may be using bandwidth
Fibre is too fast for Preseem to measure

questions.text: What is the last step in the troubleshooting flow if the issue is unresolved after all checks?
options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

questions.text:
Scenario: You have received the following communication from a client Good day to whom it may concern Name: Juan Prince. Account code: IGJAC145 My connection has been unstable for the last 2 months every night after 8pm When i watch DSTV live channels it keeps on buffering while watching rugby game, i have tired contacting your support team but nobody answers, i already rebooted the router a few times now with confirming the small black box even reseated the cables my Lan is running from the black box on the gigabyte data to the Yellow WAN of the Router and the one in Gigabyte Data power goes outside, it would appears to be connecting to the Dish outside, i have a black Router with Herotel written in white on top of the router when my rugby starts buffering its only on my tv on which i check my phone and other devices is working perfectly fine, i only have this one router on my premises when i run a speed test from my laptop which is cabled directly to my router i am getting 4 Mbps download and 2 Mbps upload from ookla speed test The smart tv is connected via Cable as well, i have also received a WhatsApp that there is Maintenance planned for next week, the only way to contact me is via email juanprincehero@consistency.key 
  • Create a ticket regarding your customers description to the helpdesk standard ticket creation 
  • Template : 
Name: 
Contact number: 
Site Location: 
Description of the customer's problem: 
Start time / Time frame: 
Rebooted Equipment (Y/N) : 
Router Type : 
Wireless or LAN devices affected : 
SSID visible (Y/N/D) : 
Devices affected :
Multiple Routers (Y/N) : 
If multiple routers – problem on all routers or certain routers : 
Confirm Power to ONU / CPE (Y/N) : 
Wire down (Y/N) : Website if access problem : 
Cables confirmed (Y/N) : 
Active outage confirmation step for the area completed (Y/N) :
questions.adminNotes:

1. added ticket template the the relevant field 
1. Fully filled in the template as per the scenario

questions.text:
Explain the following Transfer methods and there function 
- Blind Transfer
- Warm Transfer
questions.modelAnswer:
1. Blind Transfer: The call will imediatly transfer to that user or department
1. Warm Transfer: The customer will be placed on hold , then the user or department the transfer must take place to will be pulled into the communication (customer still on hold) , the reason for transfer will be explained then the communication can be transferred
