questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they are expereincing slow speed , you have confirmed the following :
  • The customer confirms that when the wireless service was installed a few years ago there were no trees in front of the direction where the antenna is pointing however in the last few months he has noticed that the trees do now seem to be at the same height as his ODU. 
  • It has also been confirmed that the speed is slow in general its not just specific times
Identify the possible cause of the problem
questions.adminNotes: • LOS is possibly obstructed between the ODU & AP / PTMP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they are in the process of downloading a file from one drive. How ever the file is 1 Gb big and only downloading at 5 Mbps. The customer has a 10 Mbps download by 5 Mbps upload package. You log into the customers antenna and confirm that the RX traffic shows 10 Mbps. What does this indicate.
questions.adminNotes: • The full download bandwidth is being utilized

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they would like to extend there network connection to a work shop on the same premises. The following has been confirmed :
  • The work shop is 300 m from the original point of instalstion 
  • You have confirmed that a ethernet cable starts degrading packets at 70 m +-
What solution can be offered to the customer to resolve this problem
questions.adminNotes: • Suggest a PTP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer brings a damaged router into the office stating it has been hit by lighting. The customer does not wish to accumalate any aditional costs and supplies a wireless extender with 2 ariels into the office to be reconfigured as the Gateway. Why will this not resolve the problem.
options: The extender cannot support the customers bandwidth
The extender cannot be configured in Gateway mode
The extender was not supplied by Herotel
The extenders make & model is not well known

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer would like to extend his Wi-Fi connectivity to his lapa by his swimming pool. The following has been confirmed :
  • The lapa is 50 m from the original instilation point
  • The connecting the lapa's equipment to the house's equipment will be exposed to weather
What is the best cabling solution for the customers request
questions.adminNotes: • STP

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following : 
  • The customer has a fiber package
  • The ONT / ONU seems to not have power as the customer states there are no lights burning on it
  • The Gateway how ever does have lights burning and there is a ethernet connection present between the ONT / ONU & the Gateway
  • There is no active data session for the radius user
What is the cause of the customer not having a active data session and why is this your answer.
questions.adminNotes:
• ONT / ONU does not have power
  • There is nothing to convert the fibre signal received from the herotel network into a copper signal so that the gateway can comunicate to and from the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in and states that recently they have been doing some construction of a building in the back of their yard. They removed a pole which connected multiple fiber customers to the Herotel network.
  • Why did removing the pole disconnect multiple customers ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The pole has a dome which connects the drop cables into the customers premises
  • The dome also connects the customers to the rest of the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacted in and confirms that there internet feels slow. When they google or try to upload something to one drive it fails and multiple attempts needs to be made before it uploads. You have confirmed the following 
  • The customer is using UTP cabling on the premises which is a office
  • All power sources for the offices originate from one point to keep the cabling neat
  • The UTP cable that connects the access point to the switch runs over the power cabling
What is the possible cause of the problems being expereinced by the customer and why do you suspect this is the cause of the problem.
questions.adminNotes:
• EMI
  • EMI is caused by high currents

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer who has 5 flats in back of his yard contacts in stating that his renters have reported slow speed & intermitted connectivity when they are connected to the Wi-Fi. You have confirmed the following :
  • Each flat has there own access point with its own SSID credentials
  • Each access point is connected via a ethernet cable to a switch in the customers home
  • The flats are 10 m apart & only one story
What could be the possible cause of the slow speed / intermitted connectivty.
questions.adminNotes: • Wireless interference

questions.text:
Scenario : A customers contacts into the First Line Support department regarding slow speed & intermitted connectivty. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customers ethernet cable between the ODU & PoE is syncing at 10 Mbps
  • The transmision method is described by the attached image
Why is this transmission method not optimal ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Half-Duplex
  • The device cannot sent & receive at the time which causes the network to feel slow

questions.text:
Scenario : A cutomer contacts in and states that they would like to configure port forwarding for their camera's that they can view them remotely. You have confirmed the following :
  • A wireless install is present on the premises
  • The ODU is configured in bridge mode
Where should the port forwarding rules be configured and why ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Gateway
  • The ODU is in bridge mode meaning the packets from the ODU pass on to the gateway , the gateway performs the natting

questions.text: Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they suspect lightning hit there ODU. You need to locate the ODU via preseem . Which universal search result do you open ?

rows: Preseem is able to receive & poll information from the OLT
This error occurs when there is a problem related to the configuration of a device. In some cases historical metrics are still populated.
Preseem is not able to poll information. If the AP / OLT is offline then the customers connected to it will also go into a offline status.
The AP / OLT's IP address has been moved out of the range that Preseem can see. The ODU / ONU connected to this AP or OLT will adopt the same status.

questions.text:
Scenario: You have received the following communication from a client Good day to whom it may concern Name: Juan Prince. Account code: IGJAC145 My connection has been unstable for the last 2 months every night after 8pm When i watch DSTV live channels it keeps on buffering while watching rugby game, i have tired contacting your support team but nobody answers, i already rebooted the router a few times now with confirming the small black box even reseated the cables my Lan is running from the black box on the gigabyte data to the Yellow WAN of the Router and the one in Gigabyte Data power goes outside, it would appears to be connecting to the Dish outside, i have a black Router with Herotel written in white on top of the router when my rugby starts buffering its only on my tv on which i check my phone and other devices is working perfectly fine, i only have this one router on my premises when i run a speed test from my laptop which is cabled directly to my router i am getting 4 Mbps download and 2 Mbps upload from ookla speed test The smart tv is connected via Cable as well, i have also received a WhatsApp that there is Maintenance planned for next week, the only way to contact me is via email juanprincehero@consistency.key 
  • Create a ticket regarding your customers description to the helpdesk standard ticket creation 
  • Template : 
Name: 
Contact number: 
Site Location: 
Description of the customer's problem: 
Start time / Time frame: 
Rebooted Equipment (Y/N) : 
Router Type : 
Wireless or LAN devices affected : 
SSID visible (Y/N/D) : 
Devices affected :
Multiple Routers (Y/N) : 
If multiple routers – problem on all routers or certain routers : 
Confirm Power to ONU / CPE (Y/N) : 
Wire down (Y/N) : Website if access problem : 
Cables confirmed (Y/N) : 
Active outage confirmation step for the area completed (Y/N) :
questions.adminNotes:
1. Created ticket with all relevant field selected  
1. added ticket template the the relevant field 
1. Fully filled in the template as per the scenario

questions.text:
Explain the following Transfer methods and there function 
- Blind Transfer
- Warm Transfer
questions.modelAnswer:
1. Blind Transfer: The call will imediatly transfer to that user or department
1. Warm Transfer: The customer will be placed on hold , then the user or department the transfer must take place to will be pulled into the communication (customer still on hold) , the reason for transfer will be explained then the communication can be transferred

questions.text: Match the scenario to the Disposition Reason

answers.2:
1. It is important to demonstrate empathy towards a customer by using the words, you understand their frustration and you apologize for any inconveniences caused.
2. Show that you're actively listening to them and not interrupt them while they are trying to report the issue so that they can feel understood and respected.
answers.5:
1. Laptop, poorly shielded electronics radiate broadband noise.
2. EMI on copper cables, LAN cable running over a section with large power will cause EMI in the cable distoring packets
answers.8: The speed test shows an available bandwidth.
answers.10: This graph is indicating that the customer is reaching their package bandwidth. The yellow line which is the average peak is spiking and the blue line which is the access point is also spiking and not stable therefore, it means that the customers AP has a high latency and that could mean the AP has a possible issue.
answers.11: If the customer is doing the speed test via WI-FI or LAN cable.
answers.13: To force a fresh channel scan, apply the new settings properly and to clear temporary glitches.
answers.15: Wireless settings for auto channel enabled needs to be changed from false to true, because the auto channel router configurations must be correct and set to true so that the router can automatically choose the best frequency band.
answers.20: This is indicating that, the yellow line, green line and blue line are spiking and are not stable. This indicates a network level bottleneck. Therefore, we can conclude that the customers latency as well as the sector is affected, this indicates a network level bottleneck and the ticket can be escalated to Config & Analyse
answers.22: I should escalate to Config & Analyse.
answers.24: Close the ticket, because the first line support troubleshooting steps have been completed and a resolution has been reached. Educate the customer about the speed test run so that when this happens again, they will know what to do before contacting the help desk support.
answers.26: I can conclude that there is a problem between the AP and the customers connected to the AP and this needs to be escalated to config & analyse.

questions.text: What is the recommended way to greet a customer on a support call?
options: Thank you for calling, this is [Name].
Thank you for calling Herotel Technical Support, [Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?
Hey, what seems to be the problem?

questions.text: What is the purpose of smiling while speaking on a call?
options: It makes your voice sound friendly and enthusiastic

questions.text: List two ways to show empathy and build rapport with the customer during a call.
questions.adminNotes:
• “I understand this must be frustrating.”
  • Use active listening and repeat their issue back.
  • Friendly tone, no sighs or frustration.

questions.text: Why do we ask the customer to test using a LAN cable?
options: Speed tests only works on LAN
To determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-specific or across all mediums

questions.text: If the customer does not have band steering enabled, which frequencies must they test?
What two physical factors around the router could affect wireless performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Router location: not behind objects, in cupboards, or stacked.
  • Avoid interference from TVs, microwaves, and fish tanks.

questions.text: Which tool is used to monitor real-time wireless throughput?
Why should we change the metrics view from 2 weeks to 15 minutes in Preseem?
options: To better match the speed test timeframe
It shows the average across all users

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • Available bandwidth at a specific moment — not the full capacity of the package.

questions.text: In Preseem, what does it mean if the yellow line meets the green line?
options: The client is experiencing packet loss
The client is maxing out their plan

questions.text: Question: What does this graph tell you about the customer’s usage?
questions.adminNotes: • Customer is using full package speed — no fault on the ISP side.

questions.text: What should you ask the customer to do before analysing throughput?
questions.adminNotes: • Ask the customer to run 3 consecutive speed tests and inform you of the times.

questions.text: What Wi-Fi setting allows the router to automatically choose the best frequency band?
Why should we ask the customer to reboot the router after enabling auto channel?
questions.adminNotes: • To apply the new wireless settings and ensure the router reselects optimal channels.

questions.text: Which wireless standard applies to the 5 GHz band?
Question: What setting needs to be changed here and why?
What must be done after changing a router setting on the ACS page?
What does good RF and good health indicate?
What should you do if RF is good but health score is bad?
What do bad RF and bad health score indicate?
options: the frequency needs to be changed to make it better
Problem between AP and client — escalate to config for further analysis.
the client needs to move the ODU to make it better

questions.text: Question: What does this tell you about the sector?
questions.adminNotes: • Sector experiencing performance issues — possibly oversubscribed or congested.

questions.text: If latency is only spiking on the client and not the sector, who do you escalate to?
options: Config and Analyze to change the clients frequency
Field Operations to check for interference or alignment problems.
Area manager to call the client and calm him down

questions.text: What should you do if both the customer and sector show unstable latency?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Config and Analyze to check channel width, power, and access point settings.

questions.text: If speed test from the sector to client is below package speed, what must be done?
When all tests are complete and no fault is found, what is your next step?
questions.adminNotes: • Educate the customer on Wi-Fi limitations, device usage, and recommend testing on LAN.

questions.text: What’s the main benefit of reviewing latency graphs on both customer and sector?
options: It tells you what equipment they have
It identifies where the fault lies

questions.text: Question: What should you conclude from this and what action is required?
questions.adminNotes: • Indicates poor signal and stability — escalate to config and possibly field ops.

rows: The customer is waiting for instilation

questions.text: Match the Following account status Meanings to the Descriptions :

questions.text: What is the differance between TCP and UDP ?
options: TCP is connection orreinted , when a packet can be sent or was received a acknowledgement takes place
Both TCP & UDP is ideal for VoIP traffic
UDP send & receives packets without acknowledgements
TCP adjusts the packets size and adjust the bandwidth speed at which packets are sent
UDP is ideal for sending a email
TCP provides the gateway with a external IP address
UDP provides the gateway with a external IP address
TCP provides error checking. Packets received are put into the correct order. In the case of a missing packet it will reqeust a re-send of that packet.

questions.text:
Scenario: You have received the following communication from a client Good day to whom it may concern Name: Juan Prince. Account code: IGJAC145 My connection has been unstable for the last 2 months every night after 8pm When i watch DSTV live channels it keeps on buffering while watching rugby game, i have tired contacting your support team but nobody answers, i already rebooted the router a few times now with confirming the small black box even reseated the cables my Lan is running from the black box on the gigabyte data to the Yellow WAN of the Router and the one in Gigabyte Data power goes outside, it would appears to be connecting to the Dish outside, i have a black Router with Herotel written in white on top of the router when my rugby starts buffering its only on my tv on which i check my phone and other devices is working perfectly fine, i only have this one router on my premises when i run a speed test from my laptop which is cabled directly to my router i am getting 4 Mbps download and 2 Mbps upload from ookla speed test The smart tv is connected via Cable as well, i have also received a WhatsApp that there is Maintenance planned for next week, the only way to contact me is via email juanprincehero@consistency.key 
  • Create a ticket regarding your customers description to the helpdesk standard ticket creation 
  • Template : 
Name: 
Contact number: 
Site Location: 
Description of the customer's problem: 
Start time / Time frame: 
Rebooted Equipment (Y/N) : 
Router Type : 
Wireless or LAN devices affected : 
SSID visible (Y/N/D) : 
Devices affected :
Multiple Routers (Y/N) : 
If multiple routers – problem on all routers or certain routers : 
Confirm Power to ONU / CPE (Y/N) : 
Wire down (Y/N) : Website if access problem : 
Cables confirmed (Y/N) : 
Active outage confirmation step for the area completed (Y/N) :
questions.adminNotes:
1. Created ticket with all relevant field selected  
1. added ticket template the the relevant field 
1. Fully filled in the template as per the scenario

questions.text:
Explain the following Transfer methods and there function 
- Blind Transfer
- Warm Transfer
questions.modelAnswer:
1. Blind Transfer: The call will imediatly transfer to that user or department
1. Warm Transfer: The customer will be placed on hold , then the user or department the transfer must take place to will be pulled into the communication (customer still on hold) , the reason for transfer will be explained then the communication can be transferred

questions.text: What is the recommended way to greet a customer when answering a call?
options: Hello, Herotel technical, how can i assist?
Good Morning, Herotel, how can i help?
Thank you for calling, Herotel Technical Support, [Agent Name] speaking. How can I assist you today?
Herotel, [Agent Name] speaking. to whom am i speaking?

questions.text: Why is it important to smile while speaking to a customer over the phone?
options: It makes your voice sound more friendly and enthusiastic.

questions.text: List two ways you can show empathy to a customer during a call.
questions.adminNotes:
• Use phrases like “I understand how frustrating that must be.”
  • Actively listen and avoid interrupting.

questions.text: Why is it important to ask a client to test using a LAN cable?
options: It helps determine if the issue is Wi-Fi-related or affects all mediums.
LAN tests are required for documentation

questions.text: If a customer does not have band steering enabled, what should they test?
What physical setup should you confirm with the customer to rule out interference?
questions.adminNotes:
• Confirm the client has clear line of sight to the router.
  • Ensure the router is not placed on top of electronics.

questions.text: Which monitoring tool is used to view real-time throughput?
Why should the metrics view be set to “Last 15 minutes”?
options: To see average usage for the month over a 15 minute interval
To compare it to previous days usage

questions.text: What does a speed test show: available bandwidth or total capacity?
questions.adminNotes: • A speed test shows available bandwidth, not total capacity.

questions.text: If a speed test shows 50 Mbps but Preseem shows 100 Mbps during the same period, what does it indicate?
options: Another device on the network is using bandwidth

questions.text: Question: What do you observe in this graph? Does it align with a typical speed test run?
questions.adminNotes:
• Identify if throughput spikes during speed test period.
  • Confirm if it matches expected package speeds.

questions.text: Explain why the upload appears later than the download in the Preseem metrics graph while doing a speed test.
questions.adminNotes: • The test runs download first, then upload — so Preseem will reflect upload slightly later.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct speed test link?
What setting must be changed in the speed test to run a 6-minute test?
questions.adminNotes:
• Set parallel connections to minimum 2
  • Set test duration to 180 seconds for both min and max

questions.text: What happens after the download portion of the 6-minute test finishes?
Question: Based on this result, is the test complete and valid? How can you tell?
questions.adminNotes:
• All speed test results are no longer greyed out.
  • Download and upload values are shown clearly.

questions.text: What setting should be enabled on the router for optimal channel selection?
Why is it beneficial to reboot the router after enabling Auto Channel?
questions.adminNotes: • It allows the router to pick the best channel based on current interference when it reboots.

questions.text: Which wireless standard operates on the 5 GHz band?
Question: What change should be made here to improve performance?
questions.adminNotes:
• Auto Channel is set to “False.”
  • Should change to “True” and commit the change.

questions.text: After changing the Auto Channel setting to “True,” what must you do next?
What is the acceptable light level range for fibre connections?
Where do you check a customer’s fibre light levels?
What action must be taken if a customer’s light level is -29 dBm?
questions.adminNotes: • Escalate to Field Operations immediately.

questions.text: If multiple clients on the same OLT have bad light levels, where do you escalate the issue?
Question: What does this indicate and where should this be escalated?
questions.adminNotes:
• Multiple bad light levels = area issue.
  • Escalate to Config and Analyze.

questions.text: Explain why a speed test may not always reflect the customer's full package speed.
questions.adminNotes: • Because the test only shows what bandwidth is available at the time — other devices may be consuming bandwidth.

questions.text: If Preseem shows full throughput, but the customer says they have no devices connected, what could be the reason?
options: A hidden device may be using bandwidth
Fibre is too fast for Preseem to measure

questions.text: What is the last step in the troubleshooting flow if the issue is unresolved after all checks?
options: Move the ticket to shift handover

questions.text: Why is it important to guide the customer through each test and configuration step instead of doing it all yourself?
questions.adminNotes:
• Ensures the customer understands the issue
  • Prevents future calls for the same issue
  • Builds trust and shows professionalism

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they are expereincing slow speed , you have confirmed the following :
  • The customer confirms that when the wireless service was installed a few years ago there were no trees in front of the direction where the antenna is pointing however in the last few months he has noticed that the trees do now seem to be at the same height as his ODU. 
  • It has also been confirmed that the speed is slow in general its not just specific times
Identify the possible cause of the problem
questions.adminNotes: • LOS is possibly obstructed between the ODU & AP / PTMP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they are in the process of downloading a file from one drive. How ever the file is 1 Gb big and only downloading at 5 Mbps. The customer has a 10 Mbps download by 5 Mbps upload package. You log into the customers antenna and confirm that the RX traffic shows 10 Mbps. What does this indicate.
questions.adminNotes: • The full download bandwidth is being utilized

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in and states that they would like to extend there network connection to a work shop on the same premises. The following has been confirmed :
  • The work shop is 300 m from the original point of instalstion 
  • You have confirmed that a ethernet cable starts degrading packets at 70 m +-
What solution can be offered to the customer to resolve this problem
questions.adminNotes: • Suggest a PTP

questions.text: Scenario : A customer brings a damaged router into the office stating it has been hit by lighting. The customer does not wish to accumalate any aditional costs and supplies a wireless extender with 2 ariels into the office to be reconfigured as the Gateway. Why will this not resolve the problem.
options: The extender cannot support the customers bandwidth
The extender cannot be configured in Gateway mode
The extender was not supplied by Herotel
The extenders make & model is not well known

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer would like to extend his Wi-Fi connectivity to his lapa by his swimming pool. The following has been confirmed :
  • The lapa is 50 m from the original instilation point
  • The connecting the lapa's equipment to the house's equipment will be exposed to weather
What is the best cabling solution for the customers request
questions.adminNotes: • STP

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacts in stating that they do not have internet access. You have confirmed the following : 
  • The customer has a fiber package
  • The ONT / ONU seems to not have power as the customer states there are no lights burning on it
  • The Gateway how ever does have lights burning and there is a ethernet connection present between the ONT / ONU & the Gateway
  • There is no active data session for the radius user
What is the cause of the customer not having a active data session and why is this your answer.
questions.adminNotes:
• ONT / ONU does not have power
  • There is nothing to convert the fibre signal received from the herotel network into a copper signal so that the gateway can comunicate to and from the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : The customer contacts in and states that recently they have been doing some construction of a building in the back of their yard. They removed a pole which connected multiple fiber customers to the Herotel network.
  • Why did removing the pole disconnect multiple customers ?
questions.adminNotes:
• The pole has a dome which connects the drop cables into the customers premises
  • The dome also connects the customers to the rest of the Herotel network

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer contacted in and confirms that there internet feels slow. When they google or try to upload something to one drive it fails and multiple attempts needs to be made before it uploads. You have confirmed the following 
  • The customer is using UTP cabling on the premises which is a office
  • All power sources for the offices originate from one point to keep the cabling neat
  • The UTP cable that connects the access point to the switch runs over the power cabling
What is the possible cause of the problems being expereinced by the customer and why do you suspect this is the cause of the problem.
questions.adminNotes:
• EMI
  • EMI is caused by high currents

questions.text:
Scenario : A customer who has 5 flats in back of his yard contacts in stating that his renters have reported slow speed & intermitted connectivity when they are connected to the Wi-Fi. You have confirmed the following :
  • Each flat has there own access point with its own SSID credentials
  • Each access point is connected via a ethernet cable to a switch in the customers home
  • The flats are 10 m apart & only one story
What could be the possible cause of the slow speed / intermitted connectivty.
questions.adminNotes: • Wireless interference

questions.text:
Scenario : A customers contacts into the First Line Support department regarding slow speed & intermitted connectivty. You have confirmed the following :
  • The customers ethernet cable between the ODU & PoE is syncing at 10 Mbps
  • The transmision method is described by the attached image
Why is this transmission method not optimal ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Half-Duplex
  • The device cannot sent & receive at the time which causes the network to feel slow

questions.text:
Scenario : A cutomer contacts in and states that they would like to configure port forwarding for their camera's that they can view them remotely. You have confirmed the following :
  • A wireless install is present on the premises
  • The ODU is configured in bridge mode
Where should the port forwarding rules be configured and why ?
questions.adminNotes:
• Gateway
  • The ODU is in bridge mode meaning the packets from the ODU pass on to the gateway , the gateway performs the natting

answers.1: The aim of showing empathy towards the customer is to build trust and rapport and it also shows professionalism.
answers.2:
1. To check if the customers CPE is powered on and all cables are fitted in correctly
2. Check if they are not experiencing any network outages
3. Check on Chip if the account is active and not suspended
answers.3: The next step is to check which installation is affected with the network outage on preseem, if only the customer is affected then I will escalate to Fields Operation, but if multiple installations are affected then I will escalate the issue to config & analyze.
answers.4: 1. I will also check if the cables are not damaged and if there is no network outages.
answers.5: The next step is to check how many customers are affected with this issue on preseem, if multiple customers are experiencing a wire down then I will escalate to config & analyze, but if only one customer is affected then I will escalate to Fields Operation, advise the customer that this is a call out, and if this is a non-herotel issue, there might be a call out fee.
answers.7: The stp cable from the antenna must be plugged in by the POE port not on the LAN port, then the ethernet cable leading to the gateway must be plugged in by the WAN port.
answers.8: When the ODU and AP are showing offline on preseem then it means there is an issue between the sector and the customers CPE, it might be that the Health Score and the RF score are critical, this means the issue must be escalated to config & analyze
answers.11: It could be that the customer is connecting using a wrong password because from the connection side, or the ethernet cable from the ONT should be connected to the blue port on the TP link Archer.
answers.14: ONT/ONU, the function of an ONT is to provide a network communication between the service provider and the customer that is connected to the network.
answers.16: They indicate that the customers router reset so the customer won't be able to connect to the wi-fi using the old SSID credentials, we would need to configure the router settings by changing the SSID username and password.
answers.17: The cause of the problem is that, the customers wi-fi is off from their device, that is why the site cannot be reached. They need to switch on the wi-fi from their device, locate the wi-fi name then enter the correct password, in that way they will be able to connect to the wi-fi and reach their sites they are trying to connect to.
